Subclause.Text: the portion, if any, of the group net excess capacity allocated to the taxpayer for the year in the joint election under paragraph (c), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: notwithstanding subparagraph (viii), the taxpayer’s excess capacity for each pre-regime year is deemed to be nil if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is a portion of the group net excess capacity that is allocated to the taxpayer or an eligible pre-regime group entity in respect of the taxpayer for a pre-regime year in the joint election under paragraph (c) is greater than the group net excess capacity, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is a portion of the group net excess capacity that is allocated to the taxpayer for a pre-regime year under the joint election is greater than the taxpayer’s net excess capacity for its pre-regime years;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an amended election is deemed to be filed in accordance with subparagraph (c)(ii) if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as a result of an assessment or reassessment, the amount of excess interest or excess capacity otherwise determined of the taxpayer, or any eligible pre-regime group entity (other than a financial institution group entity or a person exempt from tax under Part I of the Act) in respect of the taxpayer, is different from the amount reported by the taxpayer or eligible group entity in a prior election under this subsection,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the absence of the assessment or reassessment, the taxpayer’s excess capacity for each pre-regime year would not be deemed to be nil under subparagraph (d)(ix) based on a prior election, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amended election is filed within 90 days of the reassessment;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if an election or amended election has been made under paragraph (c), the Minister shall, despite subsections 152(4) and (5) of the Act, assess or reassess the tax, interest or penalties payable under the Act by any taxpayer for any relevant taxation year as is necessary to give effect to the election or amended election; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: despite paragraphs (c) and (e), the Minister may accept an election or amended election if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer and the eligible pre-regime group entities in respect of the taxpayer demonstrate to the satisfaction of the Minister that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: they made reasonable efforts to determine all amounts that may reasonably be considered relevant in making the election or amended election, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the election or amended election, as the case may be, is filed as soon as circumstances permit, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the opinion of the Minister, the circumstances are such that it would be just and equitable to permit the election to be made or amended.

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 18.3:
The following definitions apply in this section and paragraph 20(1)(yy).

Definition.Text: Canadian ordinary income, of a taxpayer for a taxation year in respect of a payment, means an amount that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer is not a partnership, included in respect of the payment in computing, in the case of a taxpayer resident in Canada, the income of the taxpayer for the purposes of this Part — or, in the case of a taxpayer that is a non-resident person, the taxable income earned in Canada of the taxpayer — for the year, except to the extent that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount is included in the Canadian ordinary income of any taxpayer under paragraph (b) or (c),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer is entitled to a deduction under section 112 or 113 in respect of the payment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount can otherwise reasonably be considered to be excluded, reduced, offset or otherwise effectively sheltered from tax under this Part by reason of any exemption, exclusion, deduction, credit (other than a credit for a tax substantially similar to tax under Part XIII) or other form of relief under this Act that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: applies specifically in respect of all or a portion of the amount and not in computing income generally, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: arises in respect of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer is a partnership, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is an amount that is included in respect of the payment in computing the income or loss of the partnership from any source, or from sources in a particular place, for the year, except to the extent that the amount

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is included in the Canadian ordinary income of any taxpayer under paragraph (c), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: can reasonably be considered to be excluded, reduced, offset or otherwise sheltered by any reason described in subparagraph (a)(iii),

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is, in respect of the partnership’s income or loss from that source or the sources in the particular place for the year,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the share of a member of the partnership that is a person resident in Canada, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the share of a member of the partnership that is a non-resident person to the extent it is included in computing the non-resident person’s taxable income earned in Canada,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the income or loss of the partnership from the source, or the sources in the particular place, for the year, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount deductible, in respect of the payment, by a member of the partnership under section 112 or 113; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula

is an amount that is included in respect of the payment in computing the foreign accrual property income of a controlled foreign affiliate of the taxpayer for a taxation year (as defined in subsection 95(1)) of the affiliate ending in the year, except to the extent the amount can reasonably be considered to be excluded, reduced, offset or otherwise effectively sheltered for any reason described in subparagraph (a)(iii), and
is the aggregate participating percentage (as defined in subsection 91(1.3)) of the taxpayer in respect of the affiliate for the taxation year of the affiliate, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer is a partnership, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is a share of the amount determined for E of a member of the partnership that is a person resident in Canada, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 1. (revenu ordinaire canadien)

Definition.Text: controlled foreign company tax regime means a set of provisions under the tax laws of a particular country other than Canada under which a direct or indirect shareholder of an entity that is located in a country other than the particular country is subject to current taxation in respect of its share of all or part of the income earned by the entity, irrespective of whether that income is distributed currently to the shareholder. (régime fiscal des sociétés étrangères contrôlées)

deductible, in relation to an amount in respect of a payment, in computing relevant foreign income or profits, includes any relief that arises in respect of the payment and is equivalent in effect to a deduction, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an exemption or exclusion in computing the relevant foreign income or profits; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a refund of, or credit that can be applied to reduce or offset, income or profits tax paid or payable to a government of a country other than Canada in respect of the relevant foreign income or profits. (déductible)

Definition.Text: entity has the same meaning as in subsection 95(1). (entité)
equity interest means any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an interest as a beneficiary under a trust;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an interest as a member of a partnership; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any similar interest in respect of any entity. (participation au capital)

Definition.Text: equity or financing return means a payment that can reasonably be considered to be in respect of, or determined by reference to,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: revenue, profit, cash flow, commodity price or any other similar criterion;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: dividends paid or payable to shareholders of any class of shares of the capital stock of a corporation, or income or capital paid or payable to any member of a partnership or beneficiary under a trust, or any other distribution in respect of any entity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that is, or is on account of, in lieu of payment of or in satisfaction of, interest, or that is otherwise compensation for the use of money. (rendement financier ou de capitaux propres)

Definition.Text: exempt dealer compensation payment means a payment that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a dealer compensation payment (as defined in subsection 260(1));

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is received by a registered securities dealer resident in Canada, as compensation for a taxable dividend paid on a share of the capital stock of a public corporation, from a non-resident corporation (referred to in this definition as the “affiliate”) that, at the time the payment is received,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a controlled foreign affiliate of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the registered securities dealer, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: another taxpayer that does not deal at arm’s length with the registered securities dealer,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a substantial market presence in a particular country other than Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: makes the payment in the ordinary course of a business of trading in securities, if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the business is carried on by the affiliate as a foreign bank (as defined in subsection 95(1)), a trust company, a credit union, an insurance corporation or a trader or dealer in securities,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the activities of the business are regulated under the laws of

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the particular country,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: another country under the laws of which the affiliate is governed and any of exists, was (unless the affiliate was continued in any jurisdiction) formed or organized, or was last continued and of each country in which the business is carried on through a permanent establishment, or

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: if the affiliate is related to a corporation, another country under the laws of which the related corporation is governed and any of exists, was (unless the related corporation was continued in any jurisdiction) formed or organized, or was last continued, if those regulating laws are recognized under the laws of the country in which the business is principally carried on and all of those countries are members of the European Union, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: conducts the business, directly or indirectly,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: principally with persons that

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: deal at arm’s length with the affiliate, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: are resident, or carry on business through a permanent establishment, in the particular country, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in competition with other entities that

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: deal at arm’s length with the affiliate, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: have a substantial market presence in the particular country; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: does not arise under, or in connection with, a structured arrangement. (paiement compensatoire (courtier) exonéré)

Definition.Text: financial instrument means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a debt;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an equity interest or any right that may reasonably be considered to replicate a right to participate in profits or gain of any entity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other arrangement that gives rise to an equity or financing return. (instrument financier)

Definition.Text: foreign expense restriction rule means a provision under the tax laws of a country other than Canada that can reasonably be considered to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: have an effect, or be intended to have an effect, that is substantially similar to that of subsection 18(4); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: have been enacted or otherwise brought into effect by the country with the intention of implementing, in whole or in part,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any of the recommendations set out in Limiting Base Erosion Involving Interest Deductions and Other Financial Payments, Action 4 – 2016 Update, published by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules set out in Tax Challenges Arising from the Digitalisation of the Economy – Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules (Pillar Two), published by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development. (règle étrangère de restriction des dépenses)

Definition.Text: foreign hybrid mismatch rule means a provision, under the tax laws of a country other than Canada, that can reasonably be considered to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: have an effect that is substantially similar to that of a provision under this section, section 12.7 or subsection 113(5); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: have been enacted or otherwise brought into effect by the country with the intention of implementing, in whole or in part, Neutralising the Effects of Hybrid Mismatch Arrangements, Action 2 – 2015 Final Report published by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development, as amended from time to time. (règle étrangère d’asymétrie hybride)

Definition.Text: foreign ordinary income, of an entity for a foreign taxation year in respect of a payment, means an amount that is determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: A − B − C − D − E − F

FormulaDefinition.Text: is an amount (referred to in this definition as the “relevant amount”) that is included in respect of the payment in computing relevant foreign income or profits of the entity for the year (other than income or profits in respect of which the entity is subject to a tax substantially similar to tax under Part XIII, or a tax under a controlled foreign company tax regime or a specified minimum tax regime) because the entity is a recipient of the payment or has a direct or indirect equity interest in a recipient of the payment;
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the relevant amount is included in computing relevant foreign income or profits in respect of which the entity is subject to an income or profits tax that is charged at a nil rate, the relevant amount, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, nil;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is any portion of the relevant amount that is included in computing relevant foreign income or profits of the entity for the year because of any foreign hybrid mismatch rule (other than any rule that is substantially similar in effect to subsection 113(5));

is any portion of the relevant amount that can reasonably be considered to be excluded, reduced, offset or otherwise effectively sheltered from income or profits tax by reason of any exemption, exclusion, deduction, credit (other than a credit for tax payable under Part XIII) or other form of relief that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: applies specifically in respect of all or a portion of the relevant amount and not in computing the entity’s relevant foreign income or profits in general, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: arises in respect of the payment;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula
is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: meets the following conditions:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is repaid or repayable in respect of income or profits tax paid or payable by the entity to the government of a country other than Canada in respect of the relevant foreign income or profits for the year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is not repaid or repayable because a loss is used to reduce or offset the relevant foreign income or profits for the year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is paid or payable in respect of a credit that can reasonably be considered to reduce or offset, directly or indirectly, the income or profits tax referred to in clause (i)(A), and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total amount of the income or profits tax referred to in clause (i)(A) of the description of G; and
is the amount determined by the formula
is the rate at which the income or profits tax referred to in clause (i)(A) in the description of G is charged in respect of the relevant amount, and

is the highest rate at which an income or profits tax imposed by the government of the country is charged in respect of an amount of income in respect of a financial instrument. (revenu ordinaire étranger)

Definition.Text: foreign taxation year of an entity means the period for which the accounts of the entity have been ordinarily made up for the purpose of computing relevant foreign income or profits of the entity, but no such period may exceed 53 weeks. (année d’imposition étrangère)
hybrid mismatch amount, in respect of a payment, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the payment arises under a hybrid financial instrument arrangement, the amount of the hybrid financial instrument mismatch in respect of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the payment arises under a hybrid transfer arrangement, the amount of the hybrid transfer mismatch in respect of the payment; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the payment arises under a substitute payment arrangement, the amount of the substitute payment mismatch in respect of the payment. (montant de l’asymétrie hybride)

Definition.Text: hybrid mismatch arrangement under which a payment arises means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a hybrid financial instrument arrangement under which the payment arises;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a hybrid transfer arrangement under which the payment arises; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a substitute payment arrangement under which the payment arises. (dispositif hybride)

Definition.Text: payer of a payment includes any entity that has an obligation to pay, credit or confer, either immediately or in the future and either absolutely or contingently, the payment to an entity. (payeur)
payment includes any amount or benefit that any entity has an obligation to pay, credit or confer, either immediately or in the future and either absolutely or contingently, to an entity. (paiement)

recipient of a payment includes any entity that has an entitlement to be paid, credited or conferred, either immediately or in the future and either absolutely or contingently, the payment by an entity. (bénéficiaire)

relevant foreign income or profits of an entity means income or profits in respect of which the entity is subject to an income or profits tax that is imposed by the government of a country other than Canada. (revenus ou bénéfices étrangers pertinents)
specified entity, in respect of another entity at any time, means a particular entity if, taking into consideration the rules in subsection (17),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the particular entity at that time, either alone or together with entities with whom the particular entity does not deal at arm’s length, owns directly or indirectly equity interests in the other entity that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: provide 25% or more of the votes that could be cast at an annual meeting of the shareholders, if the other entity is a corporation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: have 25% or more of the fair market value of all equity interests in the other entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the condition in paragraph (a) would be satisfied if the references in that paragraph to “particular entity” were read as references to “other entity” and the references to “other entity” were read as references to “particular entity”; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a third entity at that time, either alone or together with entities with which the third entity does not deal at arm’s length, owns directly or indirectly equity interests in the particular entity and the other entity that, in respect of each of the particular entity and the other entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: provide 25% or more of the votes that could be cast at an annual meeting of the shareholders, if the particular entity or the other entity, as the case may be, is a corporation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: have 25% or more of the fair market value of all equity interests in the particular entity or the other entity, as the case may be. (entité déterminée)

Definition.Text: specified minimum tax regime means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any provisions in respect of global intangible low-taxed income (as defined in section 951A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 of the United States, as amended from time to time);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any provisions under the tax laws of a country that can reasonably be considered to have been enacted or otherwise brought into effect by the country with the intention of implementing, in whole or in part, the Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules set out in Tax Challenges Arising from the Digitalisation of the Economy – Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules (Pillar Two), published by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any provisions under the tax laws of a country that can reasonably be considered to have been enacted or otherwise brought into effect by the country with the intention of implementing, in whole or in part, a Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (as defined in the model rules referred to in paragraph (b)). (régime fiscal minimum déterminé)

Definition.Text: structured arrangement means any transaction, or series of transactions, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transaction or series includes a payment that gives rise to a deduction/non-inclusion mismatch; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered, having regard to all the facts and circumstances, including the terms or conditions of the transaction or series, that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: portion of any economic benefit arising from the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch is reflected in the pricing of the transaction or series, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the transaction or series was otherwise designed to, directly or indirectly, give rise to the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch. (dispositif structuré)

Definition.Text: transaction includes an arrangement or event. (opération)

Subsection.Text: This section, section 12.7 and subsection 113(5), as well as related provisions of the Act and the Income Tax Regulations, relate to the implementation of Neutralising the Effects of Hybrid Mismatch Arrangements, Action 2 — 2015 Final Report published by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development and, unless the context otherwise requires, are to be interpreted consistently with that report, as amended from time to time.
Subsection (4) applies in respect of a payment if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the absence of this section and subsection 18(4), an amount would be deductible, in respect of the payment, in computing a taxpayer’s income from a business or property for a taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that amount is the deduction component of a hybrid mismatch arrangement under which the payment arises.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of a payment, notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, in computing a taxpayer’s income from a business or property for a taxation year, no deduction shall be made in respect of the payment to the extent of the hybrid mismatch amount in respect of the payment.

If subsection (4) or 12.7(3) would, in the absence of this subsection, apply in respect of a payment in computing a taxpayer’s income from a business or property for a taxation year, that subsection does not apply in respect of the payment if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there would be no hybrid mismatch arrangement in respect of the payment if the payment did not arise under, or in connection with, a structured arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: at the time that the taxpayer entered into, or acquired an interest in any part of a transaction that is, or is part of, the structured arrangement, it was not reasonable to expect that any of the following entities were aware of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch arising from the payment:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an entity with which the taxpayer does not deal at arm’s length, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a specified entity in respect of the taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: none of the entities described in subparagraphs (b)(i) to (iii) shared in the value of any economic benefit resulting from the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, a payment gives rise to a deduction/non-inclusion mismatch if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the following condition is met:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which would, in the absence of this section and subsection 18(4), be deductible in respect of the payment, in computing the income of a taxpayer from a business or property under this Part for a taxation year (referred to in this paragraph as the “relevant year”), and
is the total of all amounts each of which, in respect of the payment,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: can reasonably be expected to be — and actually is — foreign ordinary income of an entity for a foreign taxation year that begins on or before the day that is 12 months after the end of the relevant year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is Canadian ordinary income of a taxpayer for a taxation year that begins on or before the day that is 12 months after the end of the relevant year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the following condition is met:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which, in the absence of any foreign expense restriction rule, would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — deductible, in respect of the payment, in computing relevant foreign income or profits of an entity for a foreign taxation year (referred to in this paragraph as the “relevant foreign year”), and
is the total of all amounts, each of which, in respect of the payment,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: would, in the absence of section 12.7, be Canadian ordinary income of a taxpayer for a taxation year that begins on or before the day that is 12 months after the end of the relevant foreign year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: can reasonably be expected to be — and actually is — foreign ordinary income of another entity for a foreign taxation year that begins on or before the day that is 12 months after the end of the relevant foreign year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, if a payment gives rise to a deduction/non-inclusion mismatch,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, determined for A in paragraph (6)(a) in respect of the payment is the deduction component of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, determined for C in paragraph (6)(b) in respect of the payment is the foreign deduction component of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch arising from the payment is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if paragraph (6)(a) applies in respect of the payment, the deduction component of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if paragraph (6)(b) applies in respect of the payment, the foreign deduction component of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if subparagraph (i) of the description of A applies,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: where the amount determined for B in paragraph (6)(a) in respect of the payment is equal to 10% or less of the amount determined for A, nil, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount determined for B in paragraph (6)(a) in respect of the payment, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if subparagraph (ii) of the description of A applies,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: where the amount determined for D in paragraph (6)(b) in respect of the payment is equal to 10% or less of the amount determined for A, nil, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount determined for D in paragraph (6)(b) in respect of the payment.

Subsection.Text: Any amount that has already been included, directly or indirectly, in computing foreign ordinary income or Canadian ordinary income of a particular entity in respect of a payment shall not be included, directly or indirectly, in computing foreign ordinary income or Canadian ordinary income of the particular entity or any other entity in respect of the payment.

For the purposes of this section (other than this subsection) and section 12.7, if, in the absence of any foreign expense restriction rule, an amount (referred to in this subsection as the “deductible amount”) would be, or can reasonably be expected to be, deductible in respect of a notional interest expense on a debt in computing the relevant foreign income or profits of an entity for a foreign taxation year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the entity is deemed to make a payment in the year under the debt to the creditor in respect of the debt, in an amount equal to the deductible amount, and the creditor is deemed to be a recipient of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the deductible amount is deemed to be in respect of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is foreign ordinary income or Canadian ordinary income of the creditor in respect of notional interest income on the debt, that is calculated in respect of the same time period as the notional interest expense, is deemed to arise in respect of the payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any deduction/non-inclusion mismatch arising from the payment is deemed to satisfy the condition in paragraph (10)(d).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, a payment arises under a hybrid financial instrument arrangement if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the payment (other than a payment described in paragraphs (14)(a) to (d)) arises under, or in connection with, a financial instrument;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following conditions is satisfied:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a payer of the payment does not deal at arm’s length with, or is a specified entity in respect of, a recipient of the payment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment arises under, or in connection with, a structured arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the payment gives rise to a deduction/non-inclusion mismatch; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: arises in whole or in part because of a difference in the treatment of the financial instrument — or of one or more transactions, either alone or together, where the transaction or transactions are part of a transaction or series of transactions that includes the payment or relates to the financial instrument — for tax purposes under the laws of more than one country that is attributable to the terms or conditions of the financial instrument or transaction or transactions, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: would arise in whole or in part because of a difference described in subparagraph (i), if any other reason for the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch were disregarded.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, if a payment arises under a hybrid financial instrument arrangement,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the hybrid financial instrument mismatch, in respect of the payment, is the portion of the amount of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch arising from the payment that meets the condition in subparagraph (10)(d)(i) or (ii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the deduction component, if any, of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch is the deduction component of the hybrid financial instrument arrangement in respect of the payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the foreign deduction component, if any, of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch is the foreign deduction component of the hybrid financial instrument arrangement in respect of the payment.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, a payment (other than an exempt dealer compensation payment) arises under a hybrid transfer arrangement if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the payment arises under, or in connection with,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a transaction or series of transactions (referred to in this subsection as the “transfer arrangement”) that includes a loan or a disposition or other transfer by an entity to another entity (referred to in this subsection as the “transferor” and “transferee”, respectively) of all or a portion of a financial instrument (referred to in this subsection as the “transferred instrument”), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferred instrument;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following conditions is satisfied:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at any time during the transfer arrangement

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a payer of the payment does not deal at arm’s length with, or is a specified entity in respect of, a recipient of the payment, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the transferor does not deal at arm’s length with, or is a specified entity in respect of, the transferee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment arises under, or in connection with, a structured arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the payment gives rise to a deduction/non-inclusion mismatch; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch arises (or would arise, if any reason for the mismatch other than the reasons described in subparagraphs (i) and (ii) were disregarded), in whole or in part, because

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the payment arises as compensation for a particular payment under the transferred instrument,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the tax laws of one country treat all or a portion of the payment as though it has the same character as, or represents, the particular payment, in determining the tax consequences to an entity that is a recipient of the payment but not of the particular payment, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the tax laws of another country treat all or a portion of the payment as a deductible expense of another entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the tax laws of one country treat one or more transactions included in the transfer arrangement, either alone or together, as or as equivalent to a borrowing or other indebtedness, or treat all or a portion of the payment as arising under, or in connection with, a borrowing or other indebtedness, and the tax laws of another country do not treat the transaction or transactions, or the payment, as the case may be, in that manner, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the tax laws of one country treat the payment, or any other payment arising under, or in connection with, the transfer arrangement or transferred instrument, as though the payment or other payment, as the case may be, was derived by one entity and the tax laws of another country treat the payment or other payment, as the case may be, as though it was derived by another entity, because of a difference in how the countries treat one or more transactions included in the transfer arrangement, either alone or together.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, if a payment arises under a hybrid transfer arrangement,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the hybrid transfer mismatch, in respect of the payment, is the portion of the amount of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch arising from the payment that meets a condition in subparagraph (12)(d)(i) or (ii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the deduction component, if any, of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch is the deduction component of the hybrid transfer arrangement in respect of the payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the foreign deduction component, if any, of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch is the foreign deduction component of the hybrid transfer arrangement in respect of the payment.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, a payment arises under a substitute payment arrangement if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the payment arises under, or in connection with, an arrangement under which all or a portion of a financial instrument is loaned or disposed of or otherwise transferred by an entity to another entity (referred to in this subsection as the “transferor” and “transferee”, respectively);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee, or an entity that does not deal at arm’s length with the transferee, is a payer of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor, or an entity that does not deal at arm’s length with the transferor, is a recipient of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: all or a portion of the payment can reasonably be considered to represent or otherwise reflect, or be determined by reference to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: another payment (referred to in this subsection and subsection (15) as the “underlying return”) that arises under, or in connection with, the financial instrument, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: revenue, profit, cash flow, commodity price or any other similar criterion;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following conditions is satisfied:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at any time during that series of transactions that includes the arrangement,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a payer of the payment does not deal at arm’s length with, or is a specified entity in respect of, a recipient of the payment, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the transferor does not deal at arm’s length with, or is a specified entity in respect of, the transferee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment arises under, or in connection with, a structured arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the payment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: would give rise to a deduction/non-inclusion mismatch if any Canadian ordinary income of a taxpayer for a taxation year and any foreign ordinary income of an entity for a foreign taxation year, in respect of the payment, were limited to the portion of those amounts that can reasonably be considered to relate to the portion of the payment that is described in paragraph (d), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the condition in subparagraph (i) is not met, would meet the condition in that subparagraph if any amount that, in the absence of this section, subsection 18(4) or any foreign expense restriction rule, would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — deductible by the transferee in respect of the underlying return were instead considered to be deductible in respect of the payment, to the extent that the amount

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — deductible by the transferee in computing its income from a business or property for a taxation year or its relevant foreign income or profits for a foreign taxation year, as the case may be, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — so deductible because the underlying return accrued (or is considered to accrue) for a period before the transfer;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: one of the following conditions is satisfied:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferee or an entity that does not deal at arm’s length with the transferee is a recipient of the underlying return — or, if subparagraph (d)(ii) applies, a distribution under the financial instrument — and the amount of the underlying return or the distribution, as the case may be, exceeds the total of all amounts, in respect of the underlying return or the distribution, as the case may be, each of which can reasonably be expected to be — and actually is — foreign ordinary income for a foreign taxation year or Canadian ordinary income for a taxation year, as the case may be, of the recipient,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the condition in subparagraph (i) would be satisfied if the transferee were the recipient of the underlying return, or, if subparagraph (d)(ii) applies, a distribution under the financial instrument, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the transferor were the recipient of the underlying return, or, if subparagraph (d)(ii) applies, a distribution under the financial instrument,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: an amount in respect of the underlying return or distribution, as the case may be, would reasonably be expected to be foreign ordinary income for a foreign taxation year or Canadian ordinary income for a taxation year, as the case may be, of the transferor,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the underlying return or distribution, as the case may be, would arise under a hybrid mismatch arrangement, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: a foreign hybrid mismatch rule would reasonably be expected to apply in respect of the underlying return or distribution, as the case may be; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: one of the following entities is not resident in Canada:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferor,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferee,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a recipient of the payment,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a payer of the payment,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the issuer of the financial instrument,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a recipient of the underlying return, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: if an entity described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vi) is a partnership, a member of that entity.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, if a payment arises under a substitute payment arrangement,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the substitute payment mismatch, in respect of the payment, is the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch arising from the payment,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: if the condition in subparagraph (14)(f)(i) applies, determined based on the assumption in that subparagraph, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the condition in subparagraph (14)(f)(ii) applies, determined based on the assumption in that subparagraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the payment, or the portion of the payment, as the case may be, described in paragraph (14)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the deduction component, if any, of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch is the deduction component of the substitute payment arrangement in respect of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the foreign deduction component, if any, of the deduction/non-inclusion mismatch is the foreign deduction component of the substitute payment arrangement in respect of the payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the condition in subparagraph (14)(f)(ii) is met in respect of the payment, any amount that, in the absence of this section, subsection 18(4) or any foreign expense restriction rule, would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — deductible by the transferee in respect of the underlying return that meets the conditions in clauses (14)(f)(ii)(A) and (B) is deemed to be deductible by the transferee in respect of the payment for the purposes of applying subsections (3) and (4) and section 12.7.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, any financial instrument that is substituted for a particular financial instrument is deemed to be the particular financial instrument.
For the purposes of the definition specified entity in subsection (1), the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in determining the equity interests owned, directly or indirectly, by any entity (in this paragraph referred to as the “first entity”) in any other entity at any time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the rights of the first entity, and any entities with which it does not deal at arm’s length, that are rights referred to in the portion of the definition specified shareholder in subsection 18(5) after paragraph (b) of that definition or in paragraph (a) or (b) of the definition specified beneficiary in that subsection, or that are similar rights in respect of partnerships or any other entity, are deemed to be immediate and absolute and to have been exercised at that time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph (c) of the definition specified beneficiary in subsection 18(5) is deemed to apply at that time and the references in that definition to “particular person” are to be read as references to “first entity”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: notwithstanding paragraph (a), a particular entity is deemed not to be a specified entity in respect of another entity at any time if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular entity would, but for this paragraph, be a specified entity in respect of the other entity at that time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: there was in effect at that time an agreement or arrangement under which, on the satisfaction of a condition or the occurrence of an event that it is reasonable to expect will be satisfied or will occur, the particular entity will cease to be a specified entity in respect of the other entity, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purpose for which the particular entity became a specified entity was the safeguarding of rights or interests of the particular entity or an entity with which the particular entity is not dealing at arm’s length in respect of any indebtedness owing at any time to the particular entity or an entity with which the particular entity is not dealing at arm’s length.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, a person or partnership that is a member of a particular partnership that is a member of another partnership is deemed to be a member of the other partnership, and the person or partnership is deemed to have, directly, rights to the income or capital of the other partnership to the extent of the person or partnership’s direct and indirect rights to that income or capital.

For the purposes of this section and section 12.7, if there would, in the absence of this subsection, be multiple recipients of a particular payment, each portion of the particular payment that arises to each recipient is deemed to be a separate payment.

The tax consequences (as defined in subsection 245(1)) to a person shall be determined in order to deny a tax benefit (as defined in subsection 245(1)) to the extent necessary to eliminate any deduction/non-inclusion mismatch, or other outcome that is substantially similar to a deduction/non-inclusion mismatch, arising from a payment if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that one of the main purposes of a transaction or series of transactions that includes the payment is to avoid or limit the application of subsection (4), 12.7(3) or 113(5) in respect of the payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following conditions is met:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment is a dividend and an amount would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — deductible in respect of the payment in computing relevant foreign income or profits of an entity for a foreign taxation year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the mismatch or other outcome arises in whole or in part because of a difference in tax treatment of any transaction or series of transactions under the laws of more than one country that is attributable to the terms or conditions of the transaction or one or more transactions included in the series, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the mismatch or other outcome would arise in whole or in part because of a difference described in subparagraph (ii), if any other reason for the mismatch or other outcome were disregarded.

Subsection.Text: Each taxpayer shall file with its return of income for a taxation year a prescribed form containing prescribed information if, in computing the taxpayer’s income for the taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an amount is not deductible in respect of a payment because of subsection (4); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 12.7(3) includes an amount in respect of a payment.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition foreign expense restriction rule in subsection 18.4(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: have an effect, or be intended to have an effect, that is substantially similar to subsection 18(4) or 18.2(2); or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 18.4(3)(a) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the absence of this section and subsections 18(4) and 18.2(2), an amount would be deductible, in respect of the payment, in computing a taxpayer’s income from a business or property for a taxation year; and

Subsection.Text: The description of A in paragraph 18.4(6)(a) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which would, in the absence of this section and subsections 18(4) and 18.2(2), be deductible in respect of the payment, in computing the income of a taxpayer from a business or property under this Part for a taxation year (referred to in this paragraph as the “relevant year”), and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 18.4(14)(f)(ii) of the Act before clause (A), as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the condition in subparagraph (i) is not met, would meet the condition in that subparagraph if any amount that, in the absence of this section, subsections 18(4) and 18.2(2) or any foreign expense restriction rule, would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — deductible by the transferee in respect of the underlying return were instead considered to be deductible in respect of the payment, to the extent that the amount

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 18.4(15)(d) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the condition in subparagraph (14)(f)(ii) is met in respect of the payment, any amount that, in the absence of this section, subsections 18(4) and 18.2(2) or any foreign expense restriction rule, would be — or would reasonably be expected to be — deductible by the transferee in respect of the underlying return that meets the conditions in clauses 14(f)(ii)(A) and (B) is deemed to be deductible by the transferee in respect of the payment for the purposes of applying subsections (3) and (4) and section 12.7.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of payments arising on or after July 1, 2022, except that subsection 18.4(21) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), does not apply in respect of a payment that arises before July 1, 2023.

Subsections (2) to (6) apply in respect of taxation years of a taxpayer that begin on or after October 1, 2023. However, subsections (2) to (6) also apply in respect of a taxation year of a taxpayer that begins before, and ends after, October 1, 2023 if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any of the taxpayer’s three immediately preceding taxation years was, because of a transaction or event or a series of transactions or events, shorter than it would have been in the absence of that transaction, event or series; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that one of the purposes of the transaction, event or series was to defer the application of paragraph 12(1)(l.2) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 2(1), or the application of section 18.2 or 18.21 of the Act, as enacted by subsection 7(1), to the taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 20(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (ww), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (xx) and by adding the following after paragraph (xx):

Paragraph.Label: (yy)
Paragraph.Text: if subsection 18.4(4) has applied to deny a taxpayer a deduction, for the year or a preceding taxation year, for all or a portion of an amount in respect of a payment arising under a hybrid mismatch arrangement, and the taxpayer demonstrates that an amount is foreign ordinary income of an entity in respect of the payment (other than any amount of foreign ordinary income already taken into account in determining the amount of the deduction that was previously denied or a deduction under this paragraph) for a foreign taxation year that ends on or before the day that is 12 months after the end of the year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the lesser of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount by which the deduction that was denied exceeds the total of all amounts already deducted under this paragraph in respect of the payment for the year or any previous year, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the amount of the foreign ordinary income, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that is deductible under this paragraph is deemed to be deductible in respect of the payment.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of payments arising on or after July 1, 2022.
The portion of subparagraph 40(1)(a)(iii) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subject to subsections (1.1) to (1.3), such amount as the taxpayer may claim

Subsection.Text: Section 40 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.1):

In computing the amount that a taxpayer may claim under subparagraph (1)(a)(iii) on a disposition of shares of the capital stock of a corporation resident in Canada to another corporation, that subparagraph is to be read as if the references to “1/5” and “4” were references to “1/10” and “9” respectively, if the conditions set out in subsection 84.1(2.31) or (2.32) are satisfied in respect of the disposition.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Reserve — dispositions to employee ownership trusts
Subsection.Text: In computing the amount that a taxpayer may claim under subparagraph (1)(a)(iii) in computing the taxpayer’s gain from the disposition of a share of the capital stock of a qualifying business, that subparagraph is to be read as if the references in that subparagraph to “1/5” and “4” were references to “1/10” and “9” respectively, if the shares of the qualifying business were disposed of by the taxpayer to an employee ownership trust, or to a Canadian-controlled private corporation that is controlled and wholly-owned by an employee ownership trust, pursuant to a qualifying business transfer.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of transactions that occur on or after January 1, 2024.
Subparagraph 53(1)(e)(xiii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (xiii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount required by subsection 127(30) or section 211.92 to be added to the taxpayer’s tax otherwise payable under this Part for a taxation year that ended before that time in respect of the interest in the partnership;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 53(1)(e)(xiii) of the Act, as amended by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (xiii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount required by subsection 127(30) or 127.45(17) or section 211.92 to be added to the taxpayer’s tax otherwise payable under this Part for a taxation year that ended before that time in respect of the interest in the partnership;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 53(2)(c) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (vi):

Subparagraph.Label: (vi.1)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount equal to that portion of all amounts of a CCUS tax credit deducted under subsection 127.44(3) in computing the tax otherwise payable by the taxpayer under this Part for the taxpayer’s taxation years ending before that time that may reasonably be attributed to amounts added in computing the tax credit of the taxpayer because of subsection 127.44(11),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 53(2)(c) of the Act, as amended by subsection (3), is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (vi.1):

Subparagraph.Label: (vi.2)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount equal to that portion of all amounts of a clean technology investment tax credit deducted under subsection 127.45(6) in computing the tax otherwise payable by the taxpayer under this Part for the taxpayer’s taxation years ending before that time that may reasonably be attributed to amounts added in computing the tax credit of the taxpayer because of subsection 127.45(8),

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsections (2) and (4) are deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
Paragraphs (f.1) and (g) of the definition principal-business corporation in subsection 66(15) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: the production or marketing of calcium chloride, gypsum, kaolin, lithium, sodium chloride or potash,

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the manufacturing of products, where the manufacturing involves the processing of calcium chloride, gypsum, kaolin, lithium, sodium chloride or potash,

Subsection.Text: Section 66 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (20):

For the purposes of paragraph (f) of the definition Canadian exploration expense in subsection 66.1(6) and paragraphs (c.2) and (d) of the definition Canadian development expense in subsection 66.2(5),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a mine includes a well for the extraction of material from a lithium brine deposit;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all wells of a taxpayer for the extraction of material from one or more lithium brine deposits, the material produced from which is sent to the same plant for processing, are deemed to be one mine of the taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all wells of a taxpayer for the extraction of material from one or more lithium brine deposits that the Minister, in consultation with the Minister of Natural Resources, determines constitute one project, are deemed to be one mine of the taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
Paragraphs (c.2) and (d) of the definition Canadian development expense in subsection 66.2(5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: any expense, or portion of any expense, that is not a Canadian exploration expense, incurred by the taxpayer after March 20, 2013 for the purpose of bringing a new mine in a mineral resource in Canada, other than a bituminous sands deposit or an oil shale deposit, into production in reasonable commercial quantities and incurred before the new mine comes into production in such quantities, including an expense for clearing, removing overburden, stripping, sinking a mine shaft, constructing an adit or other underground entry or drilling a well for the extraction of lithium from brines,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any expense (other than an amount included in the capital cost of depreciable property) incurred by the taxpayer after 1987

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in sinking or excavating a mine shaft, main haulage way or similar underground work designed for continuing use, for a mine in a mineral resource in Canada built or excavated after the mine came into production,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in extending any such shaft, haulage way or work referred to in subparagraph (i), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in drilling or completing a well for the extraction of lithium from brines in Canada after the mine came into production,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of expenses incurred on or after March 28, 2023.
Subclause 66.8(1)(a)(ii)(B)(I) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts required by subsections 127(8) and 127.44(11) in respect of the partnership to be added in computing the investment tax credit or the CCUS tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)) of the taxpayer in respect of the fiscal period, and

Subsection.Text: Subclause 66.8(1)(a)(ii)(B)(I) of the Act, as amended by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts required by subsections 127(8), 127.44(11) and 127.45(8) in respect of the partnership to be added in computing the investment tax credit, the CCUS tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.44(1)) or the clean technology investment tax credit (as defined in subsection 127.45(1)) of the taxpayer in respect of the fiscal period, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
The portion of the definition commercial debt obligation in subsection 80(1) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: an amount in respect of the interest was or would have been deductible in computing the debtor’s income, taxable income or taxable income earned in Canada, as the case may be, if this Act were read without reference to paragraph 18(1)(g), subsections 18(2), (3.1) and (4) and 18.2(2) and section 21; (créance commerciale)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of taxation years of a taxpayer that begin on or after October 1, 2023.
Subsection 80.4(3) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that satisfies the conditions set out in subsection 15(2.51) and is repaid within 15 years after the qualifying business transfer referred to in that subsection.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force or is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Paragraph 84.1(2)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: notwithstanding any other paragraph in this subsection, if this paragraph applies because of subsection (2.31) or (2.32) to a disposition of subject shares by a taxpayer to a purchaser corporation, the taxpayer and the purchaser corporation are deemed to deal with each other at arm’s length at the time of the disposition of the subject shares.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 84.1(2.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of this subsection and subsections (2.31) and (2.32),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a child of a taxpayer has the same meaning as in subsection 70(10) and also includes

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a niece or nephew of the taxpayer,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a niece or nephew of the taxpayer’s spouse or common-law partner,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a spouse or common-law partner of a niece or nephew referred to in subparagraph (i) or (ii), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a child of a niece or nephew referred to in subparagraph (i) or (ii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in applying subparagraphs (2.31)(c)(iii) and (2.32)(c)(iii), if the relevant group entity is a partnership,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the partnership is deemed to be a corporation (in this paragraph referred to as the “deemed corporation”),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the deemed corporation is deemed to have a capital stock of a single class of shares, with a total of 100 issued and outstanding shares,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: each member (in this paragraph referred to as a “deemed shareholder”) of the partnership is deemed to be a shareholder of the deemed corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: each deemed shareholder of the deemed corporation is deemed to hold a number of shares in the capital stock of the deemed corporation determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is equal to

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the deemed shareholder’s specified proportion for the last fiscal period of the deemed corporation, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the deemed shareholder does not have a specified proportion described in clause (A), the proportion that is the fair market value of the deemed shareholder’s interest in the deemed corporation at that time relative to the fair market value of all interests in the deemed corporation at that time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the deemed corporation’s fiscal period is deemed to be its taxation year;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: own, directly or indirectly, in respect of a property, means

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: direct ownership of the property, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an ownership interest or, for civil law, a right in the shares of a corporation, an interest in a partnership or an interest in a trust that has a direct or indirect interest or, for civil law, a right, in the property, except that for the purposes of paragraphs (2.31)(d) and (e) and (2.32)(d) and (e), this subparagraph does not apply as a look-through rule for an interest, or for civil law, a right in non-voting preferred shares or debt of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the purchaser corporation (within the meaning of subsections (2.31) and (2.32)),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the subject corporation (within the meaning of subsections (2.31) and (2.32)), or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: any relevant group entity (within the meaning of subsections (2.31) and (2.32));

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if a person or partnership’s share of the accumulating income or capital of a trust in respect of which the person or partnership has an interest as a beneficiary depends on the exercise by a person (in this paragraph referred to as a “trustee”) of, or the failure by any trustee to exercise, a discretionary power, that trustee is deemed to have fully exercised the power, or to have failed to exercise the power, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if one or more children referred to in

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (2.31)(f)(i) have disposed of, or caused the disposition of, all of the shares in the capital stock of the purchaser corporation, the subject corporation or all relevant group entities to an arm’s length person or group of persons, the conditions set out in paragraphs (2.31)(f) and (g) are deemed to be met as of the time of the disposition, provided that all equity interests in all relevant businesses owned, directly or indirectly, by each child referred to in subparagraph (2.31)(f)(i) are included in the disposition, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (2.32)(g)(i) have disposed of, or caused the disposition of, all of the shares in the capital stock of the purchaser corporation, the subject corporation or all relevant group entities to an arm’s length person or group of persons, the conditions set out in paragraphs (2.32)(g) and (h) are deemed to be met as of the time of the disposition, provided that all equity interests in all relevant businesses owned, directly or indirectly, by each child referred to in subparagraph (2.32)(g)(i) are included in the disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if one or more children referred to in

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (2.31)(f)(i) have disposed of, or caused the disposition of, any of the shares in the capital stock of the purchaser corporation, the subject corporation or a relevant group entity to another child or group of children of the taxpayer (in this paragraph referred to as the “new child” or the “new children”), the conditions set out in paragraphs (2.31)(f) and (g) are deemed

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: to be met as of the time of the disposition, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: to continue to apply to the new child (or the new children) and any other member of the group of children that controls the subject corporation and the purchaser corporation at the time of the disposition, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (2.32)(g)(i) have disposed of, or caused the disposition of, any of the shares in the capital stock of the purchaser corporation, the subject corporation, or a relevant group entity to another child or group of children of the taxpayer (in this paragraph referred to as the “new child” or the “new children”), the conditions set out in paragraphs (2.32)(g) and (h) are deemed

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: to be met as of the time of the disposition, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: to continue to apply to the new child (or the new children) and any other member of the group of children that controls the subject corporation and the purchaser corporation at the time of the disposition;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if a child, or each of the children, referred to in

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (2.31)(f)(ii) has died or has, after the disposition of the subject shares, suffered one or more severe and prolonged impairments in physical or mental functions, the conditions set out in paragraphs (2.31)(f) and (g) are deemed to be met as of the time of the death or mental or physical impairment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph (2.32)(g)(ii) has died or has, after the disposition of the subject shares, suffered one or more severe and prolonged impairments in physical or mental functions, the conditions set out in paragraphs (2.32)(g) and (h) are deemed to be met as of the time of the death or mental or physical impairment;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if a business of a subject corporation or a relevant group entity has ceased to be carried on due to the disposition of all of the assets that were used to carry on the business in order to satisfy debts owed to creditors of the corporation or of the entity, the conditions set out in respect of the business in subparagraphs (2.31)(f)(ii) and (iii) and (2.31)(g)(i) or (2.32)(g)(ii) and (iii) and (2.32)(h)(i), as applicable, are deemed to be met as of the time of the disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: in applying paragraphs (2.31)(g) and (2.32)(h), management refers to the direction or supervision of business activities but does not include the provision of advice.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (2)(e) applies at the time of a disposition of subject shares (in this subsection referred to as the “disposition time”) by a taxpayer to a purchaser corporation if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer has not previously, at any time after 2023, sought an exception to the application of subsection (1) under paragraph (2)(e) in respect of a disposition of shares that, at that time, derived their value from an active business that is relevant to the determination of whether the subject shares satisfy the condition set out in subparagraph (b)(iii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: at the disposition time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer is an individual (other than a trust),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser corporation is controlled by one or more children (within the meaning of paragraph (2.3)(a), in this subsection referred to as the “child” or “children”) of the taxpayer, each of whom is 18 years of age or older, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the subject shares are qualified small business corporation shares or shares of the capital stock of a family farm or fishing corporation (as those terms are defined in subsection 110.6(1));

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: at all times after the disposition time, the taxpayer does not — either alone or together with a spouse or common-law partner of the taxpayer — control, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the subject corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the purchaser corporation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any other person or partnership (in this subsection referred to as a “relevant group entity”) that carries on, at the disposition time, an active business (referred to in this subsection as a “relevant business”) that is relevant to the determination of whether the subject shares satisfy the condition set out in subparagraph (b)(iii);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: at all times after the disposition time, the taxpayer does not – either alone or together with a spouse or common law partner of the taxpayer – own, directly or indirectly,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: 50% or more of any class of shares, other than shares of a specified class as defined in subsection 256(1.1) (in this subsection referred to as “non-voting preferred shares”), of the capital stock of the subject corporation or of the purchaser corporation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 50% or more of any class of equity interest (other than non-voting preferred shares) in any relevant group entity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: within 36 months after the disposition time and at all times thereafter, the taxpayer and a spouse or common-law partner of the taxpayer do not own, directly or indirectly,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any shares, other than non-voting preferred shares of the capital stock of the subject corporation or of the purchaser corporation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any equity interest (other than non-voting preferred shares) in any relevant group entity;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (2.3), from the disposition time until 36 months after that time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the child or group of children, as the case may be, controls the purchaser corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the child, or at least one member of the group of children, as the case may be, is actively engaged on a regular, continuous and substantial basis (within the meaning of paragraph 120.4(1.1)(a)) in a relevant business of the subject corporation or a relevant group entity, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: each relevant business of the subject corporation and any relevant group entity is carried on as an active business;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (2.3), within 36 months after the disposition time or such greater period as is reasonable in the circumstances, the taxpayer and a spouse or common-law partner of the taxpayer take reasonable steps to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: transfer management of each relevant business of the subject corporation and any relevant group entity to the child or at least one member of the group of children referred to in subparagraph (f)(ii), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: permanently cease to manage each relevant business of the subject corporation and any relevant group entity; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer and the child, or the taxpayer and each member of the group of children, as the case may be,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: jointly elect, in prescribed form, for paragraph (2)(e) to apply in respect of the disposition of the subject shares, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: file the election with the Minister on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for the taxation year that includes the disposition time.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (2)(e) applies at the time of a disposition of subject shares (referred to in this subsection as the “disposition time”) by a taxpayer to a purchaser corporation if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer has not previously, at any time after 2023, sought an exception to the application of subsection (1) pursuant to paragraph (2)(e) in respect of a disposition of shares that, at that time, derived their value from an active business that is relevant to the determination of whether the subject shares satisfy the condition set out in subparagraph (b)(iii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: at the disposition time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer is an individual (other than a trust),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
