Definition.Text: survivor of a holder means another individual who is, immediately before the holder’s death, a spouse or common-law partner of the holder.‍ (survivant)

Subsection.Text: The definition bénéficiaire in subsection 146.6(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: bénéficiaire Relativement à un CELIAPP, s’entend d’un particulier (y compris une succession) ou d’un donataire reconnu qui a droit à une distribution du CELIAPP après le décès du titulaire du CELIAPP. (beneficiary)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition annual FHSA limit in subsection 146.6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the FHSA carryforward for the taxation year,
is the taxpayer’s net RRSP-to-FHSA transfer amount at the end of the taxation year, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount determined in respect of each preceding taxation year that is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer had not started their maximum participation period in the year, nil, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined for E in the year, and
is the amount determined for F in the year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: $8,000 plus the amount of the FHSA carryforward for the year, and

Subsection.Text: The description of B in paragraph (b) of the definition FHSA carryforward in subsection 146.6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined in paragraph (a) of the definition annual FHSA limit for the preceding taxation year plus the total of all contributions made to a FHSA in the preceding taxation year by the taxpayer after the taxpayer’s first qualifying withdrawal from a FHSA, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 146.6(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: net RRSP-to-FHSA transfer amount of a holder at a particular time means the amount by which‍

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts transferred under paragraph 146(16)(a.2), at or before that time, to a FHSA of the holder

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts designated by the holder under paragraph (a) of the definition designated amount in subsection 207.01(1) at or before that time. (montant net de transfert de REER à CELIAPP)

Subsection.Text: Section 146.6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

An amount that is credited or added to a deposit that is a FHSA as interest or other income in respect of the FHSA is deemed not to be received by the holder of the FHSA or any other person solely because of that crediting or adding.
Subparagraph 146.6(5)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s net RRSP-to-FHSA transfer amount as at the end of the year.

Subsection.Text: The descriptions of A and B in paragraph 146.6(7)(c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount that is the total fair market value, immediately before the particular time, of all property held by a FHSA under which the last holder of the transferor FHSA is the last holder, and
is the excess FHSA amount (as defined in subsection 207.‍01(1)) of the last holder of the transferor FHSA immediately before the particular time.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 146.6(13)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the survivor is a qualifying individual at that time and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: no contributions or transfers are made to the FHSA by the survivor after that time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: no qualifying withdrawals are made from the FHSA after that time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the balance of the FHSA is transferred to a RRSP or RRIF of the survivor or distributed to the survivor in accordance with subsection (14), by the end of the year following the year of death; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the survivor is not a qualifying individual at that time, in which case the balance of the FHSA is to be transferred to a FHSA, RRSP or RRIF of the survivor, or distributed to the survivor in accordance with subsection (14), by the end of the year following the year of death.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 146.6(15)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a payment is made from the estate to a FHSA, RRSP or RRIF of the survivor, the payment is deemed to be a transfer from the FHSA to the extent that it is so designated jointly by the legal representative and the survivor in prescribed form filed with the Minister;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 146.6(17)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections (3) and (3.1) do not apply in respect of that arrangement after the particular time;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer who was the last holder under the arrangement is not deceased at the particular time, an amount equal to the fair market value of all the property of the arrangement, determined at that time, is deemed for the purposes of subsection 146.6(6) to be received at that time by the taxpayer out of or under the FHSA;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the last holder is deceased at the particular time, the proportion of the fair market value of all the property of the arrangement that a beneficiary is entitled to, determined at that time, is deemed for the purposes of subsection 146.6(14) to be distributed at that time from the FHSA to the beneficiary;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the arrangement governs a trust,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is deemed to have disposed, immediately before the particular time, of each property held by the trust for proceeds equal to the property’s fair market value immediately before the particular time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is deemed to have acquired, at the particular time, each such property at a cost equal to that fair market value,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust’s last taxation year that began before the particular time is deemed to have ended immediately before the particular time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a taxation year of the trust is deemed to begin at the particular time; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if the arrangement is a deposit or contract,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the arrangement is deemed to have been disposed of immediately before the particular time for proceeds equal to its fair market value immediately before the particular time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the arrangement is an annuity contract, the contract is deemed to be a separate annuity contract issued and effected at the particular time otherwise than pursuant to or as a FHSA, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: each person who has an interest or, for civil law, a right in the separate annuity contract or deposit, as the case may be, at the particular time is deemed to acquire the interest at the particular time at a cost equal to its fair market value at the particular time.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (11) are deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2023.
Paragraph 152(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of tax, if any, deemed by any of subsections 120(2) or (2.2), 122.5(3) to (3.003), 122.51(2), 122.7(2) or (3), 122.72(1), 122.8(4), 122.9(2), 122.91(1), 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3), 127.44(2) or 210.2(3) or (4) to be paid on account of the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 152(1)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of tax, if any, deemed by any of subsections 120(2) or (2.2), 122.5(3) to (3.003), 122.51(2), 122.7(2) or (3), 122.72(1), 122.8(4), 122.9(2), 122.91(1), 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3), 127.44(2), 127.45(2) or 210.2(3) or (4) to be paid on account of the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 152(3.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of subsections (4), (4.01), (4.2), (4.3), (4.31), (5) and (9), the normal reassessment period for a taxpayer in respect of a taxation year is
Paragraph 152(4)(b) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (vi), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (vii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (vii):

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: is made to give effect to the application of section 245 in respect of a transaction, unless the transaction was disclosed by the taxpayer to the Minister in accordance with section 237.3 or 237.4;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 152(4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.7):

Paragraph.Label: (b.8)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed form that is required to be filed under subsection 18.2(18) is not filed as and when required, and the assessment, reassessment or additional assessment is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: made before the day that is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: in the case of a taxpayer described in paragraph (3.1)(a), four years after the day on which the prescribed form containing the prescribed information is filed, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in any other case, three years after the day on which the prescribed form containing the prescribed information is filed, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of the application of paragraph 12(1)(l.2), subsection 18.2(2), clause 95(2)(f.11)(ii)(D) or (E) or paragraph 111(1)(a.1);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 152(4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.8), as enacted by subsection (5):

Paragraph.Label: (b.9)
Paragraph.Text: the assessment, reassessment or additional assessment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is made before the day that is three years after the end of the normal reassessment period for the taxpayer in respect of the year and made in respect of a disposition, in the year, of shares of the capital stock of a corporation resident in Canada in respect of which the taxpayer filed an election under paragraph 84.1(2.31)(h), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is made before the day that is 10 years after the end of the normal reassessment period for the taxpayer in respect of the year and made in respect of a disposition, in the year, of shares of the capital stock of a corporation resident in Canada in respect of which the taxpayer filed an election under paragraph 84.1(2.32)(i);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 152(4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.9), as enacted by subsection (6):

Paragraph.Label: (b.10)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed form that is required to be filed by the taxpayer, or a partnership of which the taxpayer is a member, under subsection 127.45(15) or (18) is not filed as and when required, and the assessment, reassessment or additional assessment is made in relation to transactions or events described in subsections 127.45(11) to (14) or (16) and (17) before the day that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a taxpayer described in paragraph (3.1)(a), four years after the day on which the form is filed, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, three years after the day on which the form is filed;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 152(4.01)(b) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (ix) and by adding the following after subparagraph (x):

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: the transaction referred to in subparagraph (4)(b)(viii), or

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: the transactions or events referred to in paragraph (4)(b.10);

Subsection.Text: Section 152 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Consequential assessment of Part IV tax
Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding subsections (4), (4.1) and (5), if a taxpayer in a taxation year receives a taxable dividend from a corporation that, as a result of having paid the dividend, is entitled to a dividend refund, the Minister may, within one year after the expiration of the normal reassessment period for the taxpayer in respect of the year, assess or reassess the tax, interest or penalties payable under Part IV by the taxpayer in respect of the taxable dividend.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
Subsections (3) and (9) apply to assessments or reassessments of taxpayers for taxation years that end on or after April 7, 2022.
Subsection (4) applies to transactions that occur on or after January 1, 2024.
Subsection (5) applies in respect of taxation years that begin on or after October 1, 2023.
Subsection (6) comes into force or is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subparagraph 152(4.01)(b)(xi) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (8), applies to transactions that occur on or after January 1, 2024.
Paragraph 153(1)(v) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: a payment out of or under a FHSA, if the amount is required by section 146.6 to be included in computing a taxpayer’s income

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2023.
Paragraph 157(3)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 1/12 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3) or 127.44(2) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 157(3)(e) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 1/12 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3), 127.44(2) or 127.45(2) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 157(3.1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 1/4 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3) or 127.44(2) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 157(3.1)(c) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (3), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 1/4 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3), 127.44(2) or 127.45(2) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsections (2) and (4) are deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
Section 160 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.4):
If a taxpayer and one or more other taxpayers have jointly elected under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 84.1(2.31)(h) in respect of a disposition of shares of the capital stock of a corporation resident in Canada, they are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable for the tax payable by the taxpayer under this Part to the extent that the tax payable by the taxpayer is greater than it would have been if the disposition had satisfied the conditions of subsection 84.1(2.31); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 84.1(2.32)(i) in respect of a disposition of shares of the capital stock of a corporation resident in Canada, they are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable for the tax payable by the taxpayer under this Part to the extent that the tax payable by the taxpayer is greater than it would have been if the disposition had satisfied the conditions of subsection 84.1(2.32).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force or is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subsection 160.2(2.3) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2023.
Subsection 163(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that would be deemed by subsection 127.44(2) to be paid for the year by the person if that amount were calculated by reference to the information provided in the return or form filed for the year under that subsection

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that is deemed by subsection 127.44(2) to be paid for the year by the person,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 163(2)(d.1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that would be deemed by subsection 127.44(2) or 127.45(2), as the case may be, to be paid for the year by the person if that amount were calculated by reference to the information provided in the return or form filed for the year under that subsection

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that is deemed by subsection 127.44(2) or 127.45(2), as the case may be, to be paid for the year by the person,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 183.2:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: covered entity for a taxation year, means an entity that is a corporation, trust or partnership if at any time in the taxation year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: equity of the entity is listed on a designated stock exchange; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the entity is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation resident in Canada (other than a mutual fund corporation),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a trust that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is a real estate investment trust (as defined in subsection 122.1(1)),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is a SIFT trust, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: would be a SIFT trust (other than a mutual fund trust that has one or more classes of units in continuous distribution) if

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: each reference in paragraph (a) of the definition non-portfolio property in subsection 122.1(1) to “subject entity” were read as “corporation, partnership or trust” and paragraph (c) of that definition were read without reference to the words “in Canada”,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: paragraph (a) of the definition Canadian real, immovable or resource property in subsection 248(1) were read without reference to the words “situated in Canada”, and

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the definitions timber resource property in subsection 13(21) and Canadian resource property in subsection 66(15) were read without references to the words “in Canada”, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a partnership that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is a SIFT partnership, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: would be a SIFT partnership if

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: each reference in paragraph (a) of the definition non-portfolio property in subsection 122.1(1) to “subject entity” were read as “corporation, partnership or trust” and paragraph (c) of that definition were read without reference to the words “in Canada”,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: paragraph (a) of the definition Canadian real, immovable or resource property in subsection 248(1) were read without reference to the words “situated in Canada”, and

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the definitions timber resource property in subsection 13(21) and Canadian resource property in subsection 66(15) were read without references to the words “in Canada”. (entité visée)

Definition.Text: equity of an entity, means, if the entity is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation, a share of the capital stock of the corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trust, an income or capital interest in the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership, an interest as a member of the partnership. (capitaux propres)

Definition.Text: qualifying issuance means any portion of an issuance that is made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in exchange for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: cash,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a bond, debenture, note or other security (other than equity) of the covered entity that was issued solely for cash consideration, the terms of which confer on the holder the right to make the exchange, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of properties described in subparagraph (i) or (ii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to an employee of the covered entity (or an entity related to the covered entity) in the course of the employee’s employment; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to a person or partnership, with which the covered entity deals at arm’s length and is not affiliated, in exchange for property used in the covered entity’s active business. (émission admissible)

Definition.Text: reorganization transaction means a redemption, acquisition or cancellation of equity by a covered entity that is made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on an exchange of equity by a holder for consideration that includes equity (other than substantive debt) of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the covered entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: another entity that is related to the covered entity immediately before the exchange and is a covered entity immediately after the exchange, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: another covered entity that controls the covered entity (or an amalgamated successor entity of the covered entity) immediately after the exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on an amalgamation of the covered entity with one or more other predecessor corporations to which subsection 87(1) applies if a holder of that equity, immediately before the amalgamation, receives consideration that includes equity (other than substantive debt) of the new corporation (within the meaning of subsection 87(1)) for the disposition of their equity on the amalgamation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: on a winding-up of the covered entity during which all or substantially all of the property owned by the covered entity is distributed to the equity holders of the covered entity;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the course of a reorganization to which paragraph 55(3)(a) or (b) applies;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: on a qualifying disposition (as defined in subsection 107.4(1));

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: on a qualifying exchange (as defined in subsection 132.2(1));

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: at the demand of a holder in accordance with the conditions referred to in paragraph 108(2)(a), included in the issued units of the trust, for an amount that does not exceed the fair market value of the equity at the time of the redemption, acquisition or cancellation; or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: pursuant to the exercise of a statutory right of dissent by a holder of the equity. (opération de réorganisation)

Definition.Text: specified affiliate at any time, of a covered entity, means a corporation, trust or partnership (in this definition referred to as an “affiliate”) where, at that time,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the affiliate is a corporation, the covered entity

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: controls the corporation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a direct or indirect interest in the equity of the corporation having a fair market value equal to more than 50% of the fair market value of the total equity of the corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the affiliate is a trust, the covered entity

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a majority-interest beneficiary (as defined in subsection 251.1(3)) of the trust, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a direct or indirect interest in the equity of the trust having a fair market value equal to more than 50% of the fair market value of the total equity of the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the affiliate is a partnership, the covered entity

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a majority-interest partner of the partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has a direct or indirect interest in the equity of the partnership having a fair market value equal to more than 50% of the fair market value of the total equity of the partnership. (entité affiliée déterminée)

Definition.Text: substantive debt of a covered entity means equity that, in accordance with its terms

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not convertible or exchangeable other than for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: equity that if issued would be substantive debt of the same covered entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a bond, debenture or note of the covered entity, the fair market value of which does not exceed the total of the amounts referred to in subparagraphs (d)(i) to (iv), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: equity that would be issued only after the occurrence of a trigger event pursuant to a non-viability contingent capital provision included in the terms of the equity to satisfy regulatory capital requirements applicable to the covered entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is non-voting in respect of the election of the board of directors, the trustees or the general partner (as applicable) of the covered entity, except in the event of a failure or default under the terms or conditions of the equity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: requires the amount of any dividend or other distribution payable to be calculated

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as a fixed amount, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by reference to a percentage of an amount equal to the fair market value of the consideration for which the equity was issued if the percentage is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: fixed, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: determined by reference to a market interest rate (including a Government of Canada Treasury Bill) plus a fixed amount, if any; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: entitles any holder of the equity to receive, on the redemption, cancellation or acquisition of the equity by the covered entity or by a person or partnership with whom the covered entity does not deal at arm’s length or is affiliated, an amount that does not exceed the total of the following amounts:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the fair market value of the consideration for which the equity was issued,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any unpaid distributions or dividends on the equity that are payable to the holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any premium that is payable to the holder solely due to the early redemption, cancellation or acquisition of the equity, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any other amount in respect of an amount described in subparagraphs (i) to (iii) that is attributable to an increase in the value of a currency other than Canadian currency relative to Canadian currency. (dette substantielle)

Subsection.Text: Each person or partnership that is a covered entity for a taxation year shall pay a tax for the taxation year equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total fair market value of equity (other than substantive debt) of the covered entity that is redeemed, acquired or cancelled in the taxation year by the covered entity, other than equity that is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: redeemed, acquired or cancelled in a reorganization transaction, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: acquired from a specified affiliate, if that equity was previously deemed by subsection (5) to have been acquired by the covered entity and was previously included in the description of A;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if equity of a covered entity (other than substantive debt) is redeemed, acquired or cancelled in the taxation year pursuant to a reorganization transaction described in paragraph (a) or (b) of that definition and any portion of the consideration received by a holder for the equity is not equity consideration described in paragraph (a) or (b) of the definition reorganization transaction, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total fair market value of the equity of the covered entity (other than substantive debt) that is redeemed, acquired or cancelled in a reorganization transaction described in this paragraph; and

is the total fair market value of any equity consideration described in paragraph (a) or (b) of the definition reorganization transaction that is received by a holder as consideration for the equity that is redeemed, acquired or cancelled in a reorganization transaction described in this paragraph; and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, nil; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total fair market value of equity (other than substantive debt) of the covered entity that is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: issued in a qualifying issuance in the taxation year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: disposed of in the taxation year by a specified affiliate of the covered entity (except a disposition to the covered entity or another specified affiliate of the covered entity), if that equity was previously deemed by subsection (5) to have been acquired by the covered entity and was previously included in the description of A.

Subsection.Text: Equity that is redeemed, acquired or cancelled, or that is issued by a covered entity, as part of a transaction (as defined in subsection 245(1)) or series of transactions shall be included in the description of A or B or excluded from the description of C in subsection (2) (as the case may be) if it is reasonable to consider that the primary purpose of the transaction or series is to cause a decrease in the amount referred to in the description of A or B in that subsection or an increase in the amount referred to in the description of C in that subsection.

Despite subsection (2), if the total of the amounts determined for A and B in subsection (2) for a taxation year is less than $1,000,000 (prorated based upon the number of days in the taxation year if the taxation year is less than 365 days), no tax is payable under this Part for the taxation year.

For the purposes of subsection (2), if a specified affiliate of a covered entity acquires equity of the covered entity, the equity is deemed to be acquired by the covered entity unless the specified affiliate is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a registered securities dealer that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: acquires the equity in the capacity of an agent in the ordinary course of business, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: disposes of the equity, other than to the covered entity or another specified affiliate of the covered entity, within a reasonable period of time that is consistent with the holding of equity in the ordinary course of business;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trust established for the benefit of employees and former employees of the covered entity (or of a specified affiliate of the covered entity) that satisfies the following conditions

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is an employee benefit plan, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the terms of the trust provide that any equity of the covered entity acquired or held by the trust cannot be transferred to, or otherwise be available for the benefit of, the covered entity or any specified affiliate of the covered entity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trust governed by an employees profit sharing plan; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a trust governed by a deferred profit sharing plan.

Subsection.Text: If it is reasonable to consider that one of the main purposes of a transaction (as defined in subsection 245(1)) or series of transactions is to cause a person or partnership to acquire equity of a covered entity to avoid the tax otherwise payable under this Part, the person or partnership shall be deemed to be a specified affiliate of the covered entity from the time that the transaction or series commenced until immediately after the time the transaction or series ends.
If a covered entity redeems, acquires or cancels equity of the entity in a taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the entity is a corporation, on or before the day it is required to file its return of income under Part I for the year, the corporation shall file with the Minister a return for the year under this Part in prescribed form;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the entity is a trust, within 90 days after the end of the taxation year, the trustee of the trust shall file with the Minister a return for the year under this Part in prescribed form; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the entity is a partnership, a member of the partnership that has authority to act for the partnership shall file with the Minister a return for the year under this Part in prescribed form on or before the earlier of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the day that is five months after the end of the taxation year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: March 31 in the calendar year immediately following the calendar year in which the taxation year ended.

Subsection.Text: Every covered entity that is liable to pay tax under this Part for a taxation year, shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the entity is a corporation or trust, pay its tax payable under this Part for the year to the Receiver General on or before its balance-due day for the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the entity is a partnership, pay its tax payable under this Part for the year to the Receiver General on or before the day which the partnership is required to file a return for the year under paragraph (1)(c).

Subsection.Text: Subsections 150(2) and (3), sections 152, 158 and 159, subsections 160.1(1) and 161(1) and (11), sections 162 to 167 and Division J of Part I are applicable to this Part with such modifications as the circumstances require.
Subsection (1) applies to transactions that occur after 2023.
Subparagraph (a)(iii) of the description of I in subsection 204.2(1.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount transferred to the plan on behalf of the individual in accordance with any of subsections 146(16), 146.6(7), 147(19), 147.3(1) and (4) to (7) and 147.5(21) or in circumstances to which subsection 146(21) applies,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2023.
The definition excess FHSA amount in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: excess FHSA amount of an individual at a particular time in a taxation year means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: A + B + C − D − E − F

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, if the individual had not started their maximum participation period in the preceding taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the individual’s excess FHSA amount determined at the end of the immediately preceding taxation year, in any other case;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is a contribution made to a FHSA by the individual in the taxation year at or before the particular time;
is the total of all amounts transferred in the taxation year under paragraph 146(16)(a.2), at or before the particular time, to a FHSA under which the individual is the holder;
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: $8,000 plus an amount that would have been the individual’s FHSA carryforward for the taxation year if each amount that was included in that individual’s income under subsection 146.6(6) and could have been, immediately prior to the time it was received, a designated amount, had been designated by the individual as a designated amount, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts that were deducted, could have been deducted or would have been deductible by the individual under subsection 146.6(5) in respect of all preceding taxation years if

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: no amounts were transferred under paragraph 146(16)(a.2) to a FHSA of the individual, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: notwithstanding clause (A), an amount had been contributed by the individual to a FHSA in each preceding taxation year that is the amount by which the individual’s net RRSP-to-FHSA transfer amount at the end of that year exceeds the individual’s net RRSP-to-FHSA transfer amount at the start of that year;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is a designated amount in respect of a transfer or withdrawal made by the individual in the taxation year before the particular time or an amount required to be included in computing the income of the individual under subsection 146.6(6) in the taxation year before the particular time; and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is the portion of an amount required to be included in computing the income of the individual under subsection 146.6(6) in any preceding taxation year, to the extent that it did not reduce what otherwise would have been the individual’s excess FHSA amount in any preceding taxation year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where the Minister determines that the formula in paragraph (a) does not yield an appropriate result having regard to the circumstances of the individual, a lower amount that, in the Minister’s opinion, is appropriate in the circumstances. (excédent de CELIAPP)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition designated amount in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a transfer in accordance with subparagraph 146.‍6(7)‍(b)‍(ii), to the extent that it does not exceed the total of all amounts transferred under paragraph 146(16)‍(a.‍2) to a FHSA under which the individual is the holder on or before the date of the designation less the total of all amounts previously designated under this paragraph; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition swap transaction in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (ii), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (iii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount transferred in accordance with paragraph 146(16)(a.2) or to which subsection 146.6(7) applies;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(i) of the definition swap transaction in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: both registered plans are RRIFs or RRSPs,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition swap transaction in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (iii), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iv):

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: both registered plans are FHSAs;

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) are deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2023.
Subsections (4) and (5) are deemed to have come into force on August 4, 2023.
Paragraph (a) of the definition refundable tax in subsection 207.5(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 50% of all contributions (other than an excluded contribution made on or after March 28, 2023) made under the arrangement while it was a retirement compensation arrangement and before the end of the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 207.5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: excluded contribution means an amount paid or payable under a specified arrangement to obtain or renew a letter of credit or surety bond issued by a financial institution for the purposes of securing future retirement benefit payments out of or under the arrangement; (cotisation exclue)
specified arrangement means a retirement compensation arrangement of which the primary purpose is to provide annual or more frequent periodic retirement benefit payments that are paid

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as supplemental benefits provided out of or under

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered pension plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered retirement savings plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a deferred profit sharing plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a pooled registered pension plan, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of plans described in subparagraphs (i) to (iv), or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under an arrangement that would, in the absence of subsection 147.1(8) and section 8504 of the Income Tax Regulations, substantially comply with the prescribed conditions for registration for a registered pension plan under section 8501 of those Regulations; (convention déterminée)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 207.7:
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: eligible employer means an employer that paid an amount, or that has a predecessor employer (as defined in subsection 8500(1) of the Income Tax Regulations) that paid an amount, before March 28, 2023, under a specified arrangement that is an excluded contribution. (employeur admissible)
specified refundable tax of a specified arrangement at the end of a taxation year means the amount, if any, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount elected under paragraph (2)(c); and
is the total of all amounts, if any, each of which is a refund as determined under subsection (3), in respect of a preceding taxation year. (impôt remboursable déterminé)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (3) applies to a specified arrangement if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an eligible employer, or the custodian of the arrangement, paid a refundable tax under this Part with respect to an excluded contribution made under the arrangement before March 28, 2023;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the eligible employer files an election with the Minister in prescribed form and manner; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the election includes an elected amount that does not exceed the total amount of refundable tax paid with respect to excluded contributions made under the arrangement before March 28, 2023.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies to a specified arrangement, the Minister may refund to the eligible employer, or to the custodian of the arrangement, an amount claimed on the return for a taxation year described in subsection 207.7(3), not exceeding the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 50% of all retirement benefits paid in the taxation year directly by the eligible employer for the benefit of beneficiaries whose retirement benefits were secured under the specified arrangement with a letter of credit or surety bond issued by a financial institution, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the specified refundable tax of the specified arrangement at the end of the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: If an eligible employer claims a refund under subsection (3) for a taxation year, paragraph (c) of the definition refundable tax in subsection 207.5(1) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: 50% of all amounts paid as distributions to one or more persons (including amounts that are required by paragraph 12(1)(n.3) to be included in computing the recipient’s income) under the arrangement while it was a retirement compensation arrangement and before the end of the year, other than a distribution paid where it is established, by subsequent events or otherwise, that the distribution was paid as part of a series of payments and refunds of contributions under the arrangement, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts determined under subsection 207.71(3) in respect of the specified arrangement for the year and a preceding year;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2024 and subsequent taxation years.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 211.91:
The following definitions apply in this Part and in section 127.44.

Definition.Text: actual eligible use percentage, in respect of a CCUS project, for a period means the amount, expressed as a percentage, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the quantity of captured carbon that the CCUS project supported for storage or use in eligible use during the period, and
is the total quantity of captured carbon that the CCUS project supported for storage or use in both eligible use and ineligible use during the period. (pourcentage réel d’utilisation admissible)

Definition.Text: exempt corporation at any time, means a corporation that does not have an ownership interest, whether directly or indirectly, in a qualified CCUS project in respect of which $20 million or more of qualified CCUS expenditures are expected to be incurred (based on the most recent project evaluation issued by the Minister of Natural Resources for the project). (société exonérée)

first project period, in respect of a CCUS project, means the period that begins on the first day of commercial operations — or, if the project has not yet commenced operations, the day on which, according to the most recent project plan, operations are expected to begin — and ends

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if that day is before October of a calendar year, on December 31 of the calendar year that includes the fourth anniversary of that day; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if that day is after September of a calendar year, on December 31 of the calendar year that includes the fifth anniversary of that day. (première période du projet)

Definition.Text: first recovery taxation year, in respect of a project period of a CCUS project, means the taxation year that includes the last day of the first project period. (première année d’imposition de recouvrement)
fourth project period, in respect of a CCUS project, means the five calendar years following the end of the third project period. (quatrième période du projet)

fourth recovery taxation year, in respect of a project period of a CCUS project, means the taxation year that includes the last day of the fourth project period. (quatrième année d’imposition de recouvrement)
knowledge sharing CCUS project means a qualified CCUS project that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is expected to incur qualified CCUS expenditures of $250 million or more based on the most recent project evaluation issued by the Minister of Natural Resources for the project; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has incurred $250 million or more of qualified CCUS expenditures before the first day of commercial operations of the project. (projet de CUSC requérant l’échange de connaissances)

Definition.Text: knowledge sharing report, in respect of a CCUS project, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an annual operations knowledge sharing report containing the information described by the Minister of Natural Resources in the CCUS-ITC Technical Guidance Document as published by the Minister of Natural Resources and amended from time to time, in the form annexed to the CCUS-ITC Technical Guidance Document; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the construction and completion knowledge sharing report containing the information described in the CCUS-ITC Technical Guidance Document referred to in paragraph (a). (rapport sur l’échange de connaissances)

Definition.Text: knowledge sharing taxpayer means a taxpayer that claimed a CCUS tax credit for a taxation year ending before the project start-up date of a knowledge sharing CCUS project. (contribuable échangeant des connaissances)
project period, in respect of a CCUS project, means any of the first project period, the second project period, the third project period and the fourth project period. (période de projet)
project start-up date means the day that is 120 days before the first day of commercial operations. (jour du début du projet)

recovery taxation year, in respect of a CCUS project, means any of the first recovery taxation year, the second recovery taxation year, the third recovery taxation year and the fourth recovery taxation year. (année d’imposition de recouvrement)
relevant project period means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the first recovery taxation year, the first project period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the second recovery taxation year, the second project period;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the third recovery taxation year, the third project period; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the fourth recovery taxation year, the fourth project period. (période de projet pertinente)

Definition.Text: reporting-due day means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of an annual climate risk disclosure report, the day that is nine months after the day on which the reporting taxation year for the report ends;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of an annual operations knowledge sharing report,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the report is the first such report,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: where the project start-up date is before October 1 in a calendar year, June 30 of the following calendar year, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: where the project start-up date is after September 30 in a calendar year, June 30 of the second calendar year after the calendar year which includes the the project start-up date, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the report is not the first report, each June 30 of the first four calendar years immediately following the calendar year which includes the June 30 referred to in subparagraph (i); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the construction and completion knowledge sharing report, the last day of the sixth month beginning after the project start-up date. (date d’échéance du rapport)

Definition.Text: reporting period means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of the construction and completion knowledge sharing report, the period that begins on the first day an expenditure for a CCUS project is incurred and ends on the project start-up date of the knowledge sharing CCUS project; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of an annual operations knowledge sharing report, each period that begins on the project start-up date and ends on the last day of the calendar year ending immediately before the reporting-due day for the annual operations knowledge sharing report. (période de déclaration)

Definition.Text: reporting taxation year means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the first taxation year of a taxpayer in which a CCUS tax credit was deducted, in respect of a CCUS project of the taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each taxation year that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: begins after a taxation year referred to in paragraph (a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: ends before the twenty-first calendar year after the end of the taxation year which includes the first day of commercial operations of the CCUS project. (année d’imposition de la déclaration)

Definition.Text: second project period, in respect of a CCUS project, means the five calendar years following the end of the first project period. (deuxième période du projet)

second recovery taxation year, in respect of a project period of a CCUS project, means the taxation year that includes the last day of the second project period. (deuxième année d’imposition de recouvrement)
third project period, in respect of a CCUS project, means the five calendar years following the end of the second project period. (troisième période du projet)

third recovery taxation year, in respect of a project period of a CCUS project, means the taxation year that includes the last day of the third project period. (troisième année d’imposition de recouvrement)

Subsection.Text: A taxpayer shall pay a tax under this Part, for a particular taxation year that includes the first day of commercial operations of a CCUS project, or for any preceding year, equal to the amount, if any, by which the taxpayer’s cumulative CCUS development tax credit for the immediately preceding taxation year exceeds its cumulative CCUS development tax credit for the particular taxation year.

If the actual eligible use percentage for a CCUS project for any period described in subparagraph (c)(i) or (ii) of the definition qualified CCUS project in subsection 127.44(1) is less than 10%, then for the purposes of applying subsections (4) and (5)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the actual eligible use percentage of the project for the relevant project period to which the period relates, and for each subsequent project period, is deemed to be nil;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the relevant project period for the particular recovery taxation year is deemed to include each subsequent project period; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: those subsections do not apply to a subsequent recovery taxation year in respect of the project.

Subsection.Text: If the projected eligible use percentage of a CCUS project for the relevant project period in respect of a particular recovery taxation year exceeds the actual eligible use percentage of the CCUS project for that period by more than five percentage points, there shall be added to the tax otherwise payable under this Part for the particular recovery taxation year by a taxpayer that deducted a CCUS tax credit in respect of the CCUS project an amount equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the taxpayer’s cumulative CCUS development tax credit for the taxation year that includes the first day of commercial operations;
is the amount that would be determined for A if the projected eligible use percentage for the relevant project period were equal to its actual eligible use percentage; and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount previously paid by the taxpayer as a tax under this Part in respect of the disposition or export of a property in relation to the project because of subsection (9), to the extent that the amount did not reduce the tax payable by the taxpayer under this subsection in a preceding taxation year.

Subsection.Text: If the projected eligible use percentage of a CCUS project for the relevant project period in respect of a particular recovery taxation year exceeds the actual eligible use percentage of the CCUS project for that period by more than five percentage points, there shall be added to the tax otherwise payable under this Part for the particular recovery taxation year by a taxpayer that deducted a CCUS tax credit in respect of the CCUS project, an amount equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is the amount that is the taxpayer’s CCUS refurbishment tax credit under subsection 127.44(5) for the year or a previous taxation year;
is the amount that would be determined for A if the projected eligible use percentage for the relevant project period were equal to its actual eligible use percentage; and

is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount previously paid by the taxpayer as a tax under this Part in respect of the disposition or export of a property in relation to the project because of subsection (10), to the extent that the amount did not reduce the tax payable by the taxpayer under this subsection in a preceding taxation year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining a taxpayer’s liability for tax under this Part for a taxation year, subsection (7) applies if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the actual eligible use percentage for a qualified CCUS project during a project period is significantly reduced due to extraordinary circumstances, for bona fide reasons outside the control of the taxpayer and each person or partnership that does not deal at arm’s length with the taxpayer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer requests in writing, on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for the year, that the Minister consider the potential application of this subsection and subsection (7); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister is satisfied that the taxpayer has taken all reasonable steps to attempt to rectify the extraordinary circumstances, and that it is appropriate, having regard to all the circumstances, to apply this subsection and subsection (7).

Subsection.Text: If the conditions set out in subsection (6) are met for a taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the qualified CCUS project’s operations are affected by extraordinary circumstances for all or substantially all of the project period, then no amount is payable by the taxpayer for the year under subsections (3) to (5) in respect of the project; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the portion of the project period during which the project’s operations are affected by the extraordinary circumstances shall be disregarded for the purpose of calculating the actual eligible use percentage for the project period.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining a taxpayer’s liability for tax under this Part for a recovery taxation year, if a qualified CCUS project is inoperative for all or a portion of a relevant project period,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the project is inoperative for all or substantially all of the period, then no amount is payable by the taxpayer for the year under subsections (3) to (5) in respect of the project; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the portion of the project period during which the project is inoperative shall be disregarded for the purpose of calculating the actual eligible use percentage for the project period.

Subsection.Text: Except where subsection (11) applies, if at any time in a particular taxation year a taxpayer disposes of or exports from Canada a property for which the taxpayer’s qualified CCUS expenditure resulted in the determination of a cumulative CCUS development tax credit for a previous taxation year, or would so result for the particular year but for this subsection, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the time is before the total CCUS project review period of the CCUS project to which the expenditure relates, the expenditure is deemed not to be a qualified CCUS expenditure in respect of the CCUS project for the purpose of determining the taxpayer’s cumulative CCUS development tax credit for the particular year and any subsequent taxation years; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the time is during the total CCUS project review period of the CCUS project to which the expenditure relates, there shall be added to the tax otherwise payable by the taxpayer under this Part for the year the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the qualified CCUS expenditure in respect of the property as determined for the taxation year that includes the first day of commercial operations,
is the appropriate specified percentage,
is the amount, not exceeding the amount determined for D, equal to

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is disposed of to a person who deals at arm’s length with the taxpayer, the proceeds of disposition of the property, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is disposed of to a person who does not deal at arm’s length with the taxpayer, or is exported from Canada but not disposed of, the fair market value of the property at that time,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the taxpayer’s capital cost of the property, and

is the total of all amounts, each of which can reasonably be considered to be the portion of any amount previously paid by the taxpayer because of subsection (4) in respect of the property, to the extent that the amount did not reduce the tax payable by the taxpayer under this subsection in a preceding taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Except where subsection (11) applies, if at any time in a particular taxation year during the total project review period of a CCUS project a taxpayer disposes of or removes from Canada a property for which the taxpayer’s qualified CCUS expenditure resulted in the determination of a CCUS refurbishment tax credit for the year or a previous taxation year, then there shall be added to the tax otherwise payable by the taxpayer under this Part for the year the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the qualified CCUS expenditure in respect of the property;
is the appropriate specified percentage;
is the amount, not exceeding the amount determined for D, equal to

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is disposed of to a person who deals at arm’s length with the taxpayer, the proceeds of disposition of the property, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is disposed of to a person who does not deal at arm’s length with the taxpayer, or is exported from Canada, the fair market value of the property;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the taxpayer’s capital cost of the property; and

is the total of all amounts, each of which can reasonably be considered to be the portion of any amount previously paid by the taxpayer because of subsection (5) in respect of the property, to the extent that the amount did not reduce the tax payable by the taxpayer under this subsection in a preceding taxation year.

Subsection.Text: If at any time a qualifying taxpayer (referred to in this subsection as the “vendor”) disposes of all or substantially all of its property that is part of a qualified CCUS project of the taxpayer to another taxable Canadian corporation (referred to in this subsection as the “purchaser”) and the vendor and the purchaser jointly elect in prescribed form to have this subsection apply, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the purchaser is deemed to have made the qualifying expenditures of the vendor at the times incurred by the vendor;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act that applied to the vendor in respect of the property that are relevant to the application of the Act in respect of the property after that time are deemed to have applied to the purchaser and, for greater certainty, the purchaser is deemed to have claimed the tax credits determined under section 127.44 that could have been claimed by the vendor, before that time, in respect of the CCUS project;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any project plans that were prepared or filed by the vendor in respect of the CCUS project before that time are deemed to have been filed by the purchaser;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the purchaser is or will be liable for amounts in respect of the property for which the vendor would be liable under this Part in respect of actions, transactions or events that occur after that time as if the vendor had undertaken them or otherwise participated in them; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsections (9) and (10) do not apply to the vendor in respect of the disposition of property to the purchaser.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 127.47, if subsection 127.44(11) has at any time applied to add an amount in computing the CCUS tax credit of a member of the partnership, then for the purposes of this Part, subsections (2) to (11) shall apply to determine amounts in respect of the partnership as if the partnership were a taxable Canadian corporation, its fiscal period were its taxation year and it had deducted all of the CCUS tax credits that were previously added in computing the CCUS tax credit of any member of the partnership under subsection 127.44(2) because of the application of subsection 127.44(11) in respect of its partnership interest.

Unless subsection (14) applies, if, in a taxation year, a taxpayer is a member of a partnership, the amount that can reasonably be considered to be the taxpayer’s share of any amount of tax determined because of subsection (12) in respect of the partnership for its fiscal period ending in the taxation year shall be added to the taxpayer’s tax otherwise payable under this Part for the taxation year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Election by member to pay tax
Subsection.Text: A taxable Canadian corporation that is a member of a partnership during a fiscal period of the partnership may elect, in prescribed form and manner, to add to its tax payable under this Part for its taxation year that includes the end of the fiscal period the total amount of tax determined for that fiscal period because of subsection (12) in respect of the partnership.

Each member of a partnership is jointly and severally, or for civil law, solidarily, liable for any portion of the amount of tax — determined because of subsection (12) in respect of the partnership for a taxation year — that is not added to the tax payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of a member of the partnership under subsection (13); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of a taxable Canadian corporation because of subsection (14) and paid by the corporation by its filing-due date for the year.

Subsection.Text: A taxpayer shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer is a knowledge sharing taxpayer, submit in respect of each reporting period a knowledge sharing report to the Minister of Natural Resources on or before the reporting-due day for the report; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer is a corporation that is not an exempt corporation, on or before the reporting-due day for each reporting taxation year, make available to the public, in prescribed manner, a climate risk disclosure report for the year that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: describes the climate-related risks and opportunities for the corporation based on the following thematic areas:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the corporation’s governance in respect of climate-related risks and opportunities,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the actual and potential impacts of climate-related risks and opportunities on the corporation’s businesses, strategy and financial planning, if such information is material,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the processes used by the corporation to identify, assess and manage climate related risks, and

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: the metrics and targets used by the corporation to assess and manage relevant climate-related risks and opportunities, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: explains how the corporation’s governance, strategies, policies and practices contribute to achieving Canada’s

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: commitments under the Paris Agreement made on December 12, 2015, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: goal of net-zero emissions by 2050.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), a climate risk disclosure report is deemed to have been made public in a prescribed manner if the report includes the date it was published and is made publicly available by, or on behalf of, the corporation on the website of the corporation or a related person for a period of at least three years after the reporting-due day.

If a person is required by subsection (1) to submit a knowledge sharing report in respect of a knowledge sharing CCUS project, the submission with full and accurate disclosure by any such person of the report is deemed to have been made by each person to whom subsection (1) applies in respect of the report.

Every knowledge sharing taxpayer that fails to provide the knowledge sharing report required under paragraph (1)(a) in respect of a reporting period is liable to a penalty in the amount of $2 million payable the day after the reporting-due day.

Every taxpayer that fails to make available the climate risk disclosure report as required under paragraph (1)(b) in respect of a reporting taxation year is liable to a penalty in the amount that is the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 4% of the total of all amounts, each of which is the amount of a CCUS tax credit of the corporation in respect of each taxation year that ended before the reporting-due day for the reporting taxation year, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $1 million.

Subsection.Text: The Department of Natural Resources shall publish on a website, maintained by the Government of Canada, each knowledge sharing report referred to in subsection (1) as soon as practicable after a taxpayer has submitted the report.

If a CCUS tax credit was deducted for a taxation year by a taxpayer in respect of a CCUS project that began commercial operations in the year or a prior taxation year, the actual eligible use percentage for a relevant project period in respect of the CCUS project is deemed to be nil until the taxpayer has filed in prescribed form, with each of its returns of income for taxation years that include any part of the relevant project period, a report stating

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the actual amount of carbon captured, during the calendar year ending in the taxation year, for storage or use in eligible use; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total quantity of captured carbon during that calendar year that supported storage or use in both eligible use and ineligible use.

Section.Text: Subsection 150(2) and (3), sections 152, 158, 159 and 161 to 167 and Division J of Part I apply to this Part, with such modification as the circumstances require, except that, in the application of subsection 161(1) to an amount of tax payable under section 211.92, the balance-due day of a taxpayer in respect of a recovery taxation year is deemed to be the balance-due day of the taxation year for the related CCUS tax credit under subsection 127.44(2).

Every person required by section 230 to keep records and books of account on behalf of a taxpayer shall retain all records and books of account referred to in that section as are necessary to verify information regarding CCUS tax credits of the taxpayer under section 127.44 or amounts payable by the taxpayer under this Part, in respect of a CCUS project, until the end of the later of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the period referred to in paragraph 230(4)(b), and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 26 years after the end of the taxpayer’s last taxation year for which an amount was deemed to have been paid under subsection 127.44(2) by reason of its paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
The portion of subsection 214(17) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of subsections (16) and (18),
Section 214 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (17):

For the purposes of this Part, an amount paid or credited as interest by a corporation resident in Canada in a taxation year of the corporation to a non-resident person is deemed to have been paid by the corporation as a dividend, and not to have been paid or credited by the corporation as interest, to the extent that an amount in respect of the interest is not deductible in computing the income of the corporation for the year because of subsection 18.4(4).
Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of payments arising on or after July 1, 2022.
Subsection 216(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the definitions eligible group entity, excluded entity and fixed interest commercial trust in subsection 18.2(1) and section 18.21 do not apply in computing the non-resident person’s income.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of taxation years of a taxpayer that begin on or after October 1, 2023.
Subsection 220(2.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection (2.1) does not apply in respect of a prescribed form, receipt or document, or prescribed information, that is filed with the Minister on or after the day specified, in respect of the form, receipt, document or information, in subsection 37(11), paragraph (m) of the definition investment tax credit in subsection 127(9) or subsection 127.44(17).
Subsection 220(2.2) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is replaced by the following:

Subsection (2.1) does not apply in respect of a prescribed form, receipt or document, or prescribed information, that is filed with the Minister on or after the day specified, in respect of the form, receipt, document or information, in subsection 37(11), paragraph (m) of the definition investment tax credit in subsection 127(9), subsection 127.44(17) or 127.45(3).
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on March 28, 2023.

Section.Text: Subsection 225.1(1.1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an amount payable under any of subsections 211.92(2) to (5), in respect of the day on which the notice of assessment is sent,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for one-fifth of the amount, one year after that day,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for two-fifths of the amount, two years after that day,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for three-fifths of the amount, three years after that day,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: for four-fifths of the amount, four years after that day, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: for the entire amount, five years after that day; and

Subsection.Text: Section 227 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6.2):

If, in respect of a payment (as defined in subsection 18.4(1)) arising under or in connection with a hybrid mismatch arrangement (as defined in that subsection), an amount was paid to the Receiver General under Part XIII on behalf of a person because an amount was deemed to have been paid by a corporation to the person as a dividend under subsection 214(18) and a deduction is allowed in respect of the payment or a portion of it, as the case may be, under paragraph 20(1)(yy),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (b), the Minister shall, on written application made no later than two years after the day on which the assessment is made in respect of the application of paragraph 20(1)(yy), pay to the person the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts, if any, paid to the Receiver General on or prior to the day the written application was made on behalf of the person and in respect of the liability of the person to pay an amount under Part XIII in respect of the payment or the portion of it, as the case may be, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount that would be payable to the Receiver General under Part XIII if an amount equal to the amount deductible under paragraph 20(1)(yy) were paid by the corporation to the person as a dividend described in paragraph 212(2)(a) at the end of the taxation year in which the amount is deductible under paragraph 20(1)(yy), and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount that would be payable to the Receiver General under Part XIII (if this Act were read without reference to subsection 214(18)) if an amount equal to the amount deductible under paragraph 20(1)(yy) had been paid or credited as interest by the corporation to the person at the end of the taxation year in which the amount is deductible under paragraph 20(1)(yy); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the person is or is about to become liable to make a payment to His Majesty in right of Canada, the Minister may apply the amount otherwise payable under paragraph (a) to that liability and notify the person of that action.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 227(7.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Where, on application under subsection (6.1) or (6.3) by or on behalf of a person to the Minister in respect of an amount paid under Part XIII to the Receiver General, the Minister is not satisfied that the person is entitled to the amount claimed, the Minister shall, at the person’s request, determine, with all due dispatch, the amount, if any, payable under subsection (6.1) or (6.3), as the case may be, to the person and shall send a notice of determination to the person, and sections 150 to 163, subsections 164(1) and 164(1.4) to 164(7), sections 164.1 to 167 and Division J of Part I apply with such modifications as the circumstances require.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of payments arising on or after July 1, 2022.
Section 237.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (12):

If subsection (2) does not apply to a taxpayer in respect of a transaction or series of transactions of which the transaction is a part, the taxpayer may file an information return in prescribed form and containing prescribed information in respect of the transaction or series on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for the taxation year in which the transaction occurs.
