Subsection.Text: If the length of the leave taken under any of sections 206.3 to 206.5, paragraph 206.51(3)(a) or section 206.9 is more than four weeks, the notice in writing of any change in the length of the leave shall be provided on at least four weeks’ notice, unless there is a valid reason why that cannot be done.
Subsection 207.3(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an employee who takes a leave of more than four weeks under any of sections 206.3 to 206.5 or paragraph 206.51(3)(a) wishes to shorten the length of the leave but does not provide the employer with four weeks’ notice, the employer may postpone the employee’s return to work for a period of up to four weeks after the day on which the employee informs the employer of the new end date of the leave. If the employer informs the employee that their return to work is postponed, the employee is not entitled to return to work until the day that is indicated by the employer.
Paragraph 209.4(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the absences from employment that are deemed not to have interrupted continuous employment referred to in any of sections 206.51 to 206.8;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 209.4(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing shorter periods of consecutive months of continuous employment for the purposes of subsections 206.51(6), 206.6(2), 206.7(2.1) and 206.8(1);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 210(1.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every employee who takes the leave of absence shall, as soon as possible, provide the employer with a notice in writing of the beginning of any period of leave of absence, the reasons for the leave and the length of the leave that they intend to take.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice — change in length of leave
Subsection.Text: Every employee who is on the leave of absence shall, as soon as possible, provide the employer with a notice in writing of any change in the length of the leave that they intend to take.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice — leave of more than four weeks
Subsection.Text: If the length of the leave of absence is more than four weeks, the notice in writing of any change in the length of the leave shall be provided on at least four weeks’ notice, unless there is a valid reason why that cannot be done.

If an employee who takes the leave of absence for more than four weeks wishes to shorten the length of the leave but does not provide the employer with four weeks’ notice, the employer may postpone the employee’s return to work for a period of up to four weeks after the day on which the employee informs the employer of the new end date of the leave. If the employer informs the employee that their return to work is postponed, the employee is not entitled to return to work until the day that is indicated by the employer.
The period of the postponement is deemed to be part of the leave.
Subsection 210(3) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 210:

Section.MarginalNote: Right to notice of employment opportunities
Section.Text: An employee who takes a leave of absence from employment under this Division is entitled, on written request, to be informed in writing of every employment, promotion or training opportunity that arises during the period when the employee is on the leave of absence and for which the employee is qualified, and on receiving the request, every employer of the employee shall inform the employee accordingly.

Section.MarginalNote: Resumption of employment in same position

Subsection.Text: An employee who takes a leave of absence from employment under this Division is entitled to be reinstated in the position that the employee occupied when the leave of absence commenced, and the employer of the employee shall reinstate the employee in that position at the end of the leave.

If for any valid reason an employer cannot reinstate an employee in the position referred to in subsection (1), the employer shall reinstate the employee in a comparable position with the same wages and benefits and in the same location.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Wages and benefits affected by reorganization
Subsection.Text: If an employee takes leave under this Division and, during the period of that leave, the wages and benefits of the group of employees of which that employee is a member are changed as part of a plan to reorganize the industrial establishment in which that group is employed, that employee is entitled, on being reinstated in employment under this section, to receive the wages and benefits in respect of that employment that the employee would have been entitled to receive had they been working when the reorganization took place.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of changes in wages and benefits
Subsection.Text: The employer of every employee who is on a leave of absence under this Division and whose wages and benefits would be changed as a result of a reorganization referred to in subsection (3) shall notify the employee in writing of that change as soon as possible.
The pension, health and disability benefits and the seniority of any employee who takes a leave of absence from employment under this Division accumulate during the entire period of the leave.

If contributions are required from an employee in order for the employee to be entitled to a benefit referred to in subsection (1), the employee is responsible for and shall, within a reasonable time, pay those contributions for the period of any leave of absence under this Division unless, before or within a reasonable time after taking the leave, the employee notifies the employer of the employee’s intention to discontinue contributions during that period.

An employer who pays contributions in respect of a benefit referred to in subsection (1) shall continue to pay those contributions during an employee’s leave of absence under this Division in at least the same proportion as if the employee were not on leave unless the employee does not pay the employee’s contributions, if any, within a reasonable time.

For the purposes of calculating the pension, health and disability benefits of an employee in respect of whom contributions have not been paid as required under subsections (2) and (3), the benefits do not accumulate during the leave of absence and employment on the employee’s return to work is deemed to be continuous with employment before the employee’s absence.

For the purposes of calculating benefits, other than benefits referred to in subsection (1), of an employee who takes a leave of absence under this Division, employment on the employee’s return to work is deemed to be continuous with employment before the employee’s absence.

Section.Text: Despite the provisions of any income-replacement scheme or any insurance plan in force at the workplace, an employee who takes a leave of absence from employment under this Division is entitled to benefits under the scheme or plan on the same terms as any employee who is absent from work for health-related reasons and is entitled to benefits under the scheme or plan.
No employer shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: dismiss, suspend, lay off, demote or discipline an employee because the employee applies for, intends to take or has taken a leave of absence from employment under this Division; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take into account the fact that an employee applies for, intends to take or has taken a leave of absence from employment under this Division in any decision to promote or train that employee.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: defining the expression “immediate family” for the purposes of subsection 210(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 210(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: defining the expressions “regular rate of wages” and “normal hours of work”, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: prescribing shorter periods of consecutive months of continuous employment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: specifying what does not constitute a valid reason for not reinstating an employee in the position referred to in subsection 210.2(2);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of this Division, specifying the absences from employment that are deemed not to have interrupted continuity of employment;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the circumstances in which a leave under this Division may be interrupted; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: extending the period within which a leave under this Division may be taken.

Section.Text: Subsection 246.1(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: the employer has taken action against the employee in contravention of paragraph 210.5(a) or (b);

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code

Subsection.Text: Section 6.1 of An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code is amended by replacing the subsection 210(1) that it enacts with the following:

Except when subsection (1.01) applies, every employee is entitled to and shall be granted, in the event of the death of a member of their immediate family or a family member in respect of whom the employee is, at the time of the death, on leave under section 206.3 or 206.4, a leave of absence from employment of up to 10 days that may be taken during the period that begins on the day on which the death occurs and ends six weeks after the latest of the days on which any funeral, burial or memorial service of that deceased person occurs.
Section 6.1 of the Act is amended by replacing the subsections 210(1.02) and (1.03) that it enacts with the following:
In subsection (1.01), child means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is under 18 years of age; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person in respect of whom the employee or their spouse or common-law partner, as the case may be, is entitled to the Canada caregiver credit under paragraph 118(1)(d) of the Income Tax Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 8(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Section 6.1 comes into force on the day on which section 198 of the Fall Economic Statement Implementation Act, 2023 comes into force.

Section.Text: Subsection 210(1.3) of the Canada Labour Code, as enacted by subsection 201(1), applies only with respect to leaves under section 210 of that Act that begin on or after the day on which that subsection 201(1) comes into force.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Canada Labour Code, chapter 27 of the Statutes of Canada, 2021.
If section 6.1 of the other Act comes into force before section 204 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sections 204 and 205 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 210(1) of the Canada Labour Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Except when subsection (1.01) applies, every employee is entitled to and shall be granted, in the event of the death of a member of their immediate family or a family member in respect of whom the employee is, at the time of the death, on leave under section 206.3 or 206.4, a leave of absence from employment of up to 10 days that may be taken during the period that begins on the day on which the death occurs and ends six weeks after the latest of the days on which any funeral, burial or memorial service of that deceased person occurs.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 210(1.02) and (1.03) of the Canada Labour Code are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In subsection (1.01), child means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is under 18 years of age; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person in respect of whom the employee or their spouse or common-law partner, as the case may be, is entitled to the Canada caregiver credit under paragraph 118(1)(d) of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.Text: If section 6.1 of the other Act and section 204 of this Act come into force on the same day, then that section 204 is deemed to have come into force before that section 6.1.

Section.Text: Sections 197 to 203 come into force on the 540th day after the day on which this Act receives royal assent or on an earlier day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Canada Water Agency Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act respecting the Canada Water Agency

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the importance of taking action to respond to the growing challenges threatening the health and sustainable management of freshwater ecosystems;
Whereas the Government of Canada wishes to foster collaboration with respect to freshwater issues;

Whereas the Government of Canada wishes to contribute to the protection, conservation and restoration of the quality of fresh water and the health of freshwater ecosystems in Canada and to take other collaborative measures, including the development of policy and the promotion of sound governance with respect to fresh water, as well as the improvement of the ease of access to and use of relevant data;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the importance of relying on scientific knowledge related to fresh water and of relying, through cooperation with the Indigenous peoples of Canada, on Indigenous knowledge related to fresh water;
Whereas the Government of Canada wishes to coordinate federal policies and programs with respect to freshwater issues;

Whereas the Government of Canada is committed, in the course of exercising and performing its powers, duties and functions with respect to fresh water, to fostering reconciliation with the Indigenous peoples of Canada and to ensuring respect for their rights recognized and affirmed under section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982;
Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to implementing the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples;
Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to promoting cooperation with respect to freshwater issues with provincial and territorial governments and the Indigenous peoples of Canada;
Whereas the Government of Canada wishes to promote cooperation with respect to freshwater issues with foreign governments, international organizations and interested persons and organizations;
And whereas the Government of Canada considers that the creation of the Canada Water Agency will contribute to the coordination of federal efforts to promote sustainable freshwater management;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Canada Water Agency Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Agency means the Canada Water Agency established by section 3. (Agence)
Minister means the Minister of the Environment. (ministre)
President means the President of the Agency appointed under section 7. (président)

Section.Text: The Canada Water Agency is established for the purpose of assisting the Minister in exercising or performing the Minister’s powers, duties and functions in relation to fresh water under any Act of Parliament, including the Department of the Environment Act and the Canada Water Act.
The head office of the Agency is to be at a place in Canada that is designated by the Governor in Council.
The Minister presides over the Agency and has the management and direction of it.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, subject to any terms and conditions that the Minister specifies, delegate to an officer or employee of the Agency any power, duty or function that the Minister is authorized to exercise or perform under any Act of Parliament in relation to fresh water.
However, the Minister is not authorized to delegate a power to make regulations or a power to delegate under subsection (1).

Section.Text: The President of the Agency is to be appointed by the Governor in Council to hold office during pleasure for a renewable term of up to five years.
The President is the chief executive officer of the Agency and has the rank and status of a deputy head of a department.
The President is to be paid the remuneration fixed by the Governor in Council.
The officers and employees necessary for the proper conduct of the work of the Agency are to be appointed in accordance with the Public Service Employment Act.

Subsection.Text: A department, board or agency of the Government of Canada may provide to the Agency services and facilities that are necessary for carrying out the Agency’s purpose.
In exercising its powers and performing its duties and functions, the Agency must, where appropriate, make use of those services and facilities.

Section.Text: The Agency may provide services and facilities to departments, boards and agencies of the Government of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may establish advisory committees in relation to fresh water and provide for their membership, duties, functions and operation.
The Minister may fix the remuneration that members of a committee are to be paid for the performance of their duties and functions.

The Minister may determine whether members of a committee are to be reimbursed for the travel, living and other expenses incurred in the performance of their duties and functions while absent from their ordinary place of residence. Any such reimbursement is to be paid in accordance with Treasury Board directives.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 15 to 18.

Definition.Text: former agency means the portion of the federal public administration, within the Department of the Environment, known as the Canada Water Agency. (ancienne agence)
new agency means the Canada Water Agency established by section 3. (nouvelle agence)

Subsection.Text: Nothing in this Act is to be construed as affecting the status of an employee who, immediately before the coming into force of this section, occupied a position in the former agency, except that the employee, on that coming into force, is to occupy that position in the new agency.
In subsection (1), employee has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Public Service Employment Act.

Section.Text: Any amount that is appropriated by an Act of Parliament, for the fiscal year in which this section comes into force, to defray the expenditures of the former agency and that is unexpended on the day on which this section comes into force is deemed to be an amount appropriated to defray the expenditures of the new agency.

Section.MarginalNote: Transfer of powers, duties and functions
Section.Text: Any power, duty or function that is exercisable by an officer or employee of the former agency under any Act, order, rule or regulation or under any contract, lease, licence or other document, is to be exercised by the appropriate officer or employee of the new agency.

For greater certainty, the powers, duties and functions referred to in section 17 include those related to the administration, in whole or in part, of any contract, lease, licence or other document that relates to the activities, management or operation of the former agency.
Schedule I to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding the following, in alphabetical order, under the heading “Other Government Institutions”:
Schedule I.1 to the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order in column I, a reference to

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference in column II to “Minister of the Environment.”

Section.Text: Schedule IV to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:
Part II of Schedule VI to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order in column I, a reference to

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference in column II to “President.”

Section.Text: The schedule to the Privacy Act is amended by adding the following, in alphabetical order, under the heading “Other Government Institutions”:
Part I of Schedule I to the Public Service Superannuation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:
The provisions of the Canada Water Agency Act, as enacted by section 209, and sections 210 to 215, come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Tobacco and Vaping Products Act is amended by adding the following after section 42:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make regulations respecting fees or charges to be paid by manufacturers for the purpose of recovering the costs incurred by His Majesty in right of Canada in relation to the carrying out of the purpose of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fixing the fees or charges or providing for the manner of calculating them;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: requiring manufacturers to submit to the Minister information for the calculation of the fees or charges and prescribing the information that manufacturers must submit as well as the form and manner in which and the time within which the information must be submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the payment of the fees or charges, including the time and manner of payment;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of section 42.13, the information that the Minister must make available to the public, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the name of each manufacturer who is required to pay the fees or charges,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: information relating to whether each manufacturer has paid the fees or charges,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: information relating to whether each manufacturer has submitted the information required under this Part, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: information relating to the measures taken in respect of each manufacturer who has failed to pay the fees or charges or submit the information required under this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that by this Part is to be prescribed.

Subsection.Text: Before making regulations, the Minister must consult with any persons or entities that the Minister considers to be interested in the matter.
The Minister may, by order, remit all or part of any fee or charge provided for under this Part or the interest on it.
A remission may be conditional.
If a remission is conditional and the condition is not fulfilled, then the remission is cancelled and is deemed never to have been granted.
Every manufacturer must keep, in the prescribed manner and for the prescribed time, all documents that they used in order to submit the information required under this Part to the Minister.
The manufacturer must keep the documents at their place of business in Canada or at any prescribed place and must, on written request, provide them to the Minister.

Section.Text: The Minister must make available to the public, within the prescribed time, the information relating to the fees and charges provided for under this Part that is required by the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Any fees or charges payable under this Part constitute a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in a court of competent jurisdiction.
No proceedings to recover a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada under subsection (1) may be commenced later than five years after the day on which the debt became payable.
Any debt that may be recovered under subsection 42.14(1) in respect of which there is a default of payment, or the part of any such debt that has not been paid, may be certified by the Minister.

On production to the Federal Court, a certificate made under subsection (1) must be registered in that Court and, when registered, has the same force and effect, and all proceedings may be taken on the certificate, as if it were a judgment obtained in that Court for a debt of the amount specified in the certificate and all reasonable costs and charges attendant in the registration of the certificate.

The Minister may, by order, prohibit the sale of a tobacco product or vaping product by a manufacturer who fails to pay the fees or charges payable under this Part or submit the information required under this Part.
An order made under subsection (1) is not a statutory instrument within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 46:

Section.MarginalNote: Offences related to fees and charges
Section.Text: Every manufacturer who contravenes subsection 42.12(1) or (2) or an order made under subsection 42.16(1) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $50,000.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition central cooperative credit society and central in subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Canadian Payments Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whose directors are wholly or primarily individuals elected or appointed by local cooperative credit societies; (société coopérative de crédit centrale ou centrale)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: entity includes a corporation, trust, partnership, fund, agency and unincorporated association or organization; (entité)
person includes an entity; (personne)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a central, a trust company, a loan company, a local and any other person that accepts deposits transferable by order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a clearing house, as defined in section 2 of the Payment Clearing and Settlement Act, of a clearing and settlement system designated under subsection 4(1) of that Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (g), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (h) and by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a payment service provider, as defined in section 2 of the Retail Payment Activities Act, that performs retail payment activities, as defined in that section.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 9(1) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No individual is eligible to be a director if they are
Subparagraph 18(1)(k)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the remuneration of directors referred to in paragraph 8(1)(d) and of individuals referred to in subsection 21.2(7),

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 18(1)(k)(iii) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the procedures for the nomination, selection and appointment of individuals to be members of the Stakeholder Advisory Council or the Member Advisory Council.

Section.Text: Subsection 20(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Le conseil constitue un comité de nomination chargé de désigner des candidats compétents et de proposer leur candidature à l’élection d’administrateurs.

Section.Text: Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Board may, subject to the regulations, establish other committees consisting of such individuals as the Board considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 21.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
There shall be a Stakeholder Advisory Council consisting of individuals who are independent of the Association and of its members and are appointed by the Board in consultation with the Minister.
Subsection 21.2(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Council shall be broadly representative of users and payment service providers that are not members of the Association.
The portion of subsection 21.2(7) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Association may pay the remuneration that is fixed by by-law to the following individuals:
Paragraph 21.2(7)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any individual who represents the interests of such a member or who is represented by such a member.

Section.Text: Subsection 21.4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: There shall be a Member Advisory Council consisting of individuals appointed by the Board.

Section.Text: Paragraph 35(1)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the election of directors of the Association, including the eligibility of individuals to be elected as directors, and defining independent for the purposes of paragraph 8(1)(d);

Section.Text: Paragraph 40(1)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions an entity must meet to become a participant in the designated payment system;

Section.Text: Section 49 of the Act is replaced by the following:

On the fourth anniversary of the day on which this section comes into force, the Minister shall cause to be conducted a review of this Act and its operation and cause a report on the review to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the review is completed.
Sections 219 to 228 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsections 19(4) and (5) of the Competition Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A judge of a superior or county court in the province in which a record placed in custody under this section was ordered to be produced or in which it was found, or of the Federal Court, sitting in camera, may decide the question of solicitor-client privilege in relation to the record on application made in accordance with the rules of the court by the Commissioner or the owner of the record or the person in whose possession it was found if notice of the application has been given by the applicant to all other persons entitled to make application.

Section.Text: Section 45.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No proceedings may be commenced under subsection 45(1) or (1.1) against a person on the basis of facts that are the same or substantially the same as the facts on the basis of which an order against that person is sought by the Commissioner under section 76, 79, 90.1 or 92.
Subsection 52(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No proceedings may be commenced under this section against a person against whom an order is, on application by the Commissioner, sought under Part VII.1 on the basis of the same or substantially the same facts as would be alleged in proceedings under this section.

Section.Text: Subsection 52(1.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, the making of a representation of a price that is not attainable due to fixed obligatory charges or fees constitutes a false or misleading representation, unless the obligatory charges or fees represent only an amount imposed on a purchaser of the product referred to in subsection (1) by or under an Act of Parliament or the legislature of a province.
Section 52.01 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For greater certainty, the making of a representation of a price that is not attainable due to fixed obligatory charges or fees constitutes a false or misleading representation, unless the obligatory charges or fees represent only an amount imposed on a purchaser of the product referred to in subsections (1) to (3) by or under an Act of Parliament or the legislature of a province.
Subsection 52.01(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

No proceedings may be commenced under this section against a person on the basis of facts that are the same or substantially the same as the facts on the basis of which an order against that person is, on application by the Commissioner, sought under Part VII.1.

Section.Text: Subsection 67(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Corporations — trials with or without jury
Subsection.Text: Despite anything in the Criminal Code or in any other statute or law, the following rules apply to corporations charged with an offence under this Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if one or more corporations are charged and no individual is charged in the same indictment, the corporation or corporations are to be tried without a jury;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if one or more corporations and a single individual are charged in the same indictment, then, unless the court is satisfied that the ends of justice require otherwise, the corporation or corporations are to be tried

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: without a jury if the individual elects or re-elects to be tried without a jury, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: with a jury if the individual elects or re-elects to be tried with a jury; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if one or more corporations and two or more individuals are charged in the same indictment, then, unless the court is satisfied that the ends of justice require otherwise, the corporation or corporations are to be tried

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: without a jury if all the individuals elect or re-elect to be tried without a jury,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: with a jury if all the individuals elect or re-elect to be tried with a jury, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: either with or without a jury, as determined by the Attorney General of Canada for each corporation, if some but not all of the individuals elect or re-elect to be tried without a jury.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 74.01(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: makes a representation to the public in the form of a statement, warranty or guarantee of a product’s benefits for protecting or restoring the environment or mitigating the environmental, social and ecological causes or effects of climate change that is not based on an adequate and proper test, the proof of which lies on the person making the representation;

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: makes a representation to the public with respect to the benefits of a business or business activity for protecting or restoring the environment or mitigating the environmental and ecological causes or effects of climate change that is not based on adequate and proper substantiation in accordance with internationally recognized methodology, the proof of which lies on the person making the representation; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 74.01(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For greater certainty, the making of a representation of a price that is not attainable due to fixed obligatory charges or fees constitutes a false or misleading representation, unless the obligatory charges or fees represent only an amount imposed on a purchaser of the product referred to in subsection (1) by or under an Act of Parliament or the legislature of a province.
Subsection 74.01(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person engages in reviewable conduct who, for the purpose of promoting, directly or indirectly, the supply or use of a product or for the purpose of promoting, directly or indirectly, any business interest, by any means, makes a representation to the public as to the price at which a product or like products have been, are or will be ordinarily supplied by the person making the representation unless that person, having regard to the nature of the product and the relevant geographic market, establishes that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they have sold a substantial volume of the product at that price or a higher price within a reasonable period of time before or after the making of the representation, as the case may be; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: they have offered the product at that price or a higher price in good faith for a substantial period of time recently before or immediately after the making of the representation, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Section 74.011 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, the making of a representation of a price that is not attainable due to fixed obligatory charges or fees constitutes a false or misleading representation, unless the obligatory charges or fees represent only an amount imposed on a purchaser of the product referred to in subsections (1) to (3) by or under an Act of Parliament or the legislature of a province.
For greater certainty, in determining whether or not the person who made the representation engaged in the reviewable conduct, it is not necessary to establish that any person was deceived or misled.

Section.Text: Section 74.09 of the Act is replaced by the following:
In sections 74.1 to 74.14 and 74.18, court means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of an application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal, the Federal Court or the superior court of a province; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of an application made by a person granted leave under section 103.1, the Tribunal.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 74.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Determination of reviewable conduct and judicial order

Subsection.Text: If, on application by the Commissioner or a person granted leave under section 103.1, a court determines that a person is engaging in or has engaged in reviewable conduct under this Part, the court may order the person
The portion of subsection 74.1(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph (1)(c), an order made against a person in respect of conduct that is reviewable under paragraph 74.01(1)(a), (b), (b.1) or (c), subsection 74.01(2) or (3) or section 74.011, 74.02, 74.04, 74.05 or 74.06 is a subsequent order if
Paragraph 74.1(6)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an order in respect of conduct reviewable under paragraph 74.01(1)(a) or section 74.011, the person was previously convicted of an offence under section 52, or under paragraph 52(1)(a) as it read immediately before the coming into force of this Part; or

Subsection.Text: Section 74.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):

In considering an application by a person granted leave under section 103.1, the Tribunal may not draw any inference from the fact that the Commissioner has or has not taken any action in respect of the matter raised by the application.

Section.Text: Section 74.11 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On application by the Commissioner or a person granted leave under section 103.1, a court may order a person who it appears to the court is engaging in conduct that is reviewable under this Part not to engage in that conduct or substantially similar reviewable conduct if it appears to the court that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: serious harm is likely to ensue unless the order is issued; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the balance of convenience favours issuing the order.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Temporary order — supply of a product
Subsection.Text: On application by the Commissioner or a person granted leave under section 103.1, a court may order any person named in the application to refrain from supplying to another person a product that it appears to the court is or is likely to be used to engage in conduct that is reviewable under this Part, or to do any act or thing that it appears to the court could prevent a person from engaging in such conduct, if it appears to the court that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: serious harm is likely to ensue unless the order is issued; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the balance of convenience favours issuing the order.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (5), an order made under subsection (1) or (1.1) has effect, or may be extended on application by the Commissioner or a person granted leave under section 103.1, for any period that the court considers sufficient to meet the circumstances of the case.

Subject to subsection (4), at least 48 hours’ notice of an application referred to in subsection (1), (1.1) or (2) must be given by or on behalf of the Commissioner or the person granted leave under section 103.1 to the person in respect of whom the order or extension is sought.

The court may proceed ex parte with an application made by the Commissioner under subsection (1) or (1.1) if it is satisfied that subsection (3) cannot reasonably be complied with or that the urgency of the situation is such that service of notice in accordance with subsection (3) would not be in the public interest.

An order issued ex parte as the result of an application made by the Commissioner under subsection (1) or (1.1) has effect for the period that is specified in it, not exceeding seven days unless, on further application made by the Commissioner on notice as provided in subsection (3), the court extends the order for any additional period that it considers necessary and sufficient.

If an order issued under this section as the result of an application made by the Commissioner under subsection (1) or (1.1) is in effect, the Commissioner must proceed as expeditiously as possible to complete the inquiry under section 10 arising out of the conduct in respect of which the order was issued.
Subsections 74.111(1) to (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If, on application by the Commissioner or a person granted leave under section 103.1, a court finds a strong prima facie case that a person is engaging in or has engaged in conduct that is reviewable under paragraph 74.01(1)(a), and the court is satisfied that the person owns or has possession or control of articles within the jurisdiction of the court and is disposing of or is likely to dispose of them by any means, and that the disposal of the articles will substantially impair the enforceability of an order made under paragraph 74.1(1)(d), the court may issue an interim injunction forbidding the person or any other person from disposing of or otherwise dealing with the articles, other than in the manner and on the terms specified in the injunction.

Any application for an injunction under subsection (1) must include a statement that the Commissioner or the person granted leave under section 103.1 has applied for an order under paragraph 74.1(1)(d), or that the Commissioner or the person intends to apply for an order under that paragraph if the Commissioner or the person applies for an order under paragraph 74.1(1)(a).

Subject to subsection (6), the injunction has effect, or may be extended on application by the Commissioner or the person granted leave under section 103.1, for any period that the court considers sufficient to meet the circumstances of the case.

Subject to subsection (5), at least 48 hours’ notice of an application referred to in subsection (1) or (3) must be given by or on behalf of the Commissioner or the person granted leave under section 103.1 to the person in respect of whom the injunction or extension is sought.

The court may proceed ex parte with an application made by the Commissioner under subsection (1) if it is satisfied that subsection (4) cannot reasonably be complied with or that the urgency of the situation is such that service of the notice in accordance with subsection (4) might defeat the purpose of the injunction or would otherwise not be in the public interest.

An injunction issued ex parte as the result of an application made by the Commissioner under subsection (1) has effect for the period that is specified in it, not exceeding seven days unless, on further application made by the Commissioner on notice as provided in subsection (4), the court extends the injunction for any additional period that it considers sufficient.
Subsection 74.111(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an injunction issued under this section as the result of an application made by the Commissioner under subsection (1) is in effect, the Commissioner must proceed as expeditiously as possible to complete any inquiry under section 10 arising out of the conduct in respect of which the injunction was issued.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 74.12:

Section.MarginalNote: Failure to comply with consent agreement

Subsection.Text: If, on application by the Commissioner, the court determines that a person, without good and sufficient cause, the proof of which lies on the person, has failed to comply or is likely to fail to comply with a consent agreement registered under subsection 74.12(3), the court may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prohibit the person from doing anything that, in the court’s opinion, may constitute a failure to comply with the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to take any action that is necessary to comply with the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to pay, in any manner that the court specifies, an administrative monetary penalty in an amount not exceeding $10,000 for each day on which they fail to comply with the agreement, determined by the court after taking into account any evidence of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s financial position,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s history of compliance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the duration of the period of non-compliance, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any other relevant factor; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: grant any other relief that the court considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: The terms of an order under paragraph (1)(c) are to be determined with a view to promoting conduct by the person that is in conformity with the purposes of this Act and not with a view to punishment.
The administrative monetary penalty imposed under paragraph (1)(c) is a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such from the person in a court of competent jurisdiction.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 74.13:

Section.MarginalNote: Consent agreement — parties to a private action

Subsection.Text: If a person granted leave under section 103.1 makes an application to the Tribunal for an order under section 74.1 and the terms of the order are agreed to by the person in respect of whom the order is sought and consistent with the provisions of this Act, a consent agreement may be filed with the Tribunal for registration.
On filing the consent agreement with the Tribunal for registration, the parties shall serve a copy of it on the Commissioner without delay.
The consent agreement must be published without delay in the Canada Gazette.

The consent agreement must be registered within 30 days after its publication unless a third party makes an application to the Tribunal before then to cancel the agreement or replace it with an order of the Tribunal.
On registration, the consent agreement has the same force and effect, and proceedings may be taken, as if it were an order of the Tribunal.
On application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal may vary or rescind a registered consent agreement if it finds that the agreement is not in conformity with the purposes of this Part.
The Commissioner must give notice of an application under subsection (6) to the parties to the consent agreement.

Section.MarginalNote: Failure to comply with consent agreement

Subsection.Text: If, on application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal determines that a person, without good and sufficient cause, the proof of which lies on the person, has failed to comply or is likely to fail to comply with a consent agreement registered under subsection 74.131(4), the Tribunal may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prohibit the person from doing anything that, in the Tribunal’s opinion, may constitute a failure to comply with the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to take any action that is necessary to comply with the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to pay, in any manner that the Tribunal specifies, an administrative monetary penalty in an amount not exceeding $10,000 for each day on which they fail to comply with the agreement, determined by the Tribunal after taking into account any evidence of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s financial position,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s history of compliance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the duration of the period of non-compliance, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any other relevant factor; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: grant any other relief that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: The terms of an order under paragraph (1)(c) are to be determined with a view to promoting conduct by the person that is in conformity with the purposes of this Act and not with a view to punishment.
The administrative monetary penalty imposed under paragraph (1)(c) is a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such from the person in a court of competent jurisdiction.

If a person granted leave under section 103.1 makes an application to the Tribunal for an order under section 74.1 and the person discontinues the application by reason of having entered into an agreement with any other person, the parties to the agreement must serve a copy of it on the Commissioner within 10 days after the day on which it is entered into.
On application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal may vary or rescind the agreement if it finds that the agreement is not in conformity with the purposes of this Part.
The Commissioner must give notice of an application under subsection (2) to the parties to the agreement.

If, on application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal determines that a person, without good and sufficient cause, the proof of which lies on the person, has failed to serve a copy of an agreement on the Commissioner in accordance with subsection 74.133(1), the Tribunal may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to serve the Commissioner with a copy of the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: issue an interim order prohibiting any person from doing anything that, in the Tribunal’s opinion, may constitute or be directed toward the implementation of the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to pay, in any manner that the Tribunal specifies, an administrative monetary penalty in an amount not exceeding $10,000 for each day on which they fail to serve a copy of the agreement on the Commissioner, determined by the Tribunal after taking into account any evidence of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s financial position,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s history of compliance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the duration of the period of non-compliance, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any other relevant factor; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: grant any other relief that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: The terms of an order under paragraph (1)(c) are to be determined with a view to promoting conduct by the person that is in conformity with the purposes of this Act and not with a view to punishment.
The administrative monetary penalty imposed under paragraph (1)(c) is a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such from the person in a court of competent jurisdiction.
Subsection 75(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Jurisdiction of Tribunal — cases of refusal to deal

Subsection.Text: The Tribunal may, on application by the Commissioner or a person granted leave under section 103.1, order one or more suppliers of a product, including a means of diagnosis or repair, in a market to accept a person as a customer, or to make the means of diagnosis or repair available to a person, within a specified period and on the terms that the Tribunal considers appropriate if the Tribunal finds that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is substantially affected in the whole or part of their business or is precluded from carrying on business due to their inability to obtain adequate supplies of the product anywhere in the market on usual trade terms;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person is unable to obtain adequate supplies of the product because of insufficient competition among suppliers of the product in the market;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person is willing and able to meet the usual trade terms of the supplier or suppliers of the product;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the product is in ample supply or, in the case of a means of diagnosis or repair, can be readily supplied; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the refusal to deal is having or is likely to have an adverse effect on competition in a market.

Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection (1) does not apply in the case of an article if, within the specified time, any customs duties on the article are removed, reduced or remitted and the effect of the removal, reduction or remission is to place the person on an equal footing with other persons who are able to obtain adequate supplies of the article in Canada.
Section 75 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.1):

If the Tribunal makes an order under subsection (1) as the result of an application by a person granted leave under section 103.1, it may also order any supplier in respect of whom the order applies to pay an amount, not exceeding the value of the benefit derived from the conduct that is the subject of the order, to be distributed among the applicant and any other person affected by the conduct, in any manner that the Tribunal considers appropriate.
The Tribunal may specify in an order made under subsection (1.2) any term that it considers necessary for the order’s implementation, including a term

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: specifying how the payment is to be administered;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the appointment of an administrator to administer the payment and specifying the terms of administration;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: requiring the person against whom the order is made to pay the administrative costs related to the payment as well as the fees to be paid to an administrator;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: requiring that potential claimants be notified in the time and manner specified by the Tribunal;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the time and manner for making claims;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the conditions for the eligibility of claimants, including conditions relating to the return of the products to the person against whom the order is made; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the manner in which, and the terms on which, any amount of the payment that remains unclaimed or undistributed is to be dealt with.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 75(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Nothing in this section is to be interpreted as requiring the disclosure of any information that is a trade secret.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: means of diagnosis or repair includes diagnostic, maintenance, repair and calibration information, technical updates, diagnostic software or tools and any related documentation and service parts. (moyen de diagnostic ou de réparation)
trade terms means terms in respect of payment, units of purchase and reasonable technical and servicing requirements. (conditions de commerce)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 76(11)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order against that person has been made under section 79 or 90.1.

Subsection.Text: Section 76 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (11):

If the Tribunal makes an order under subsection (2) as the result of an application by a person granted leave under section 103.1, it may also order the person against whom the order is made to pay an amount, not exceeding the value of the benefit derived from the conduct that is the subject of the order, to be distributed among the applicant and any other person affected by the conduct, in any manner that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

The Tribunal may specify in an order made under subsection (11.1) any term that it considers necessary for the order’s implementation, including any term referred to in any of paragraphs 75(1.3)(a) to (g).

Section.Text: Subsection 77(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Tribunal makes an order under subsection (2) or (3) as the result of an application by a person granted leave under section 103.1, it may also order any supplier in respect of whom the order applies to pay an amount, not exceeding the value of the benefit derived from the conduct that is the subject of the order, to be distributed among the applicant and any other person affected by the conduct, in any manner that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

The Tribunal may specify in an order made under subsection (3.1) any term that it considers necessary for the order’s implementation, including any term referred to in any of paragraphs 75(1.3)(a) to (g).
Subsection 79(3.2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: the amount that the person against whom the order is made is required to pay under an order made under subsection (4.1);

Subsection.Text: Section 79 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

If, as the result of an application by a person granted leave under section 103.1, the Tribunal makes an order under subsection (1) or (2), it may also order the person against whom the order is made to pay an amount, not exceeding the value of the benefit derived from the practice that is the subject of the order, to be distributed among the applicant and any other person affected by the practice, in any manner that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

The Tribunal may specify in an order made under subsection (4.1) any term that it considers necessary for the order’s implementation, including any term referred to in any of paragraphs 75(1.3)(a) to (g).
Paragraph 79(7)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order against that person has been made under section 76, 90.1 or 92.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 90.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, on application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal finds that an agreement or arrangement or a proposed agreement or arrangement between persons of whom two or more are competitors prevents or lessens, has prevented or lessened or is likely to prevent or lessen competition substantially in a market, the Tribunal may make an order
The portion of subsection 90.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, on application by the Commissioner or a person granted leave under section 103.1, the Tribunal finds that an agreement or arrangement or a proposed agreement or arrangement between persons of whom two or more are competitors prevents or lessens, has prevented or lessened or is likely to prevent or lessen competition substantially in a market, the Tribunal may make an order
Section 90.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

If, on an application under subsection (1), the Tribunal finds that an agreement or arrangement has had or is having the effect of preventing or lessening competition substantially in a market and that an order under subsection (1) is not likely to restore competition in that market, the Tribunal may, in addition to or in lieu of making an order under subsection (1), make an order directing any or all the persons against whom an order is sought to take actions, including the divestiture of assets or shares, that are reasonable and as are necessary to overcome the effects of the agreement or arrangement in that market.

In making an order under subsection (1.1), the Tribunal shall make the order in such terms as will in its opinion interfere with the rights of any person to whom the order is directed or any other person affected by it only to the extent necessary to achieve the purpose of the order.

If the Tribunal makes an order against a person under subsection (1) or (1.1), it may also order them to pay, in any manner that the Tribunal specifies, an administrative monetary penalty in an amount not exceeding the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $10,000,000 and, for each subsequent order under either of those subsections, an amount not exceeding $15,000,000, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: three times the value of the benefit derived from the agreement or arrangement, or, if that amount cannot be reasonably determined, 3% of the person’s annual worldwide gross revenues.

Subsection.Text: In determining the amount of an administrative monetary penalty, the Tribunal shall take into account any evidence of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the effect on competition in the relevant market;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the gross revenue from sales affected by the agreement or arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any actual or anticipated profits affected by the agreement or arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the financial position of the person against whom the order is made;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the history of compliance with this Act by the person against whom the order is made; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other relevant factor.

Subsection.Text: The purpose of an order made against a person under subsection (1.3) is to promote practices by that person that are in conformity with the purposes of this section and not to punish that person.
Subsection 90.1(1.4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that the person against whom the order is made is required to pay under an order made under subsection (10.1);

Subsection.Text: Section 90.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):
No application may be made under this section in respect of an agreement or arrangement that has been terminated for more than three years.

The administrative monetary penalty imposed on a person under subsection (1.3) is a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such from that person in a court of competent jurisdiction.
Paragraph 90.1(10)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order against that person has been made under section 76, 79 or 92.

Subsection.Text: Section 90.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (10):

If the Tribunal makes an order under subsection (1) as the result of an application by a person granted leave under section 103.1, it may also order the person against whom the order is made to pay an amount, not exceeding the value of the benefit derived from the conduct that is the subject of the order, to be distributed among the applicant and any other person affected by the conduct, in any manner that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

The Tribunal may specify in an order made under subsection (10.1) any term that it considers necessary for the order’s implementation, including any term referred to in any of paragraphs 75(1.3)(a) to (g).

In considering an application by a person granted leave under section 103.1, the Tribunal may not draw any inference from the fact that the Commissioner has or has not taken any action in respect of the matter raised by the application.
Paragraphs 92(1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: among the sources from which a trade, industry or profession obtains a product, including labour,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: among the outlets through which a trade, industry or profession disposes of a product, including labour, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 92(1)(e) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a completed merger, in order to restore competition to the level that would have prevailed but for the merger, order any party to the merger or any other person

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 92(1)(f) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a proposed merger, in order to preserve the level of competition that would prevail but for the merger, make an order directed against any party to the proposed merger or any other person

Subsection.Text: Clause 92(1)(f)(iii)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: prohibiting the person against whom the order is directed, should the merger or part thereof be completed, from doing any act or thing the prohibition of which the Tribunal determines to be necessary to ensure that the merger or part thereof does not prevent or lessen competition, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 92(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of this section, if the Tribunal finds, on a balance of probabilities, that a merger or proposed merger results or is likely to result in a significant increase in concentration or market share, the Tribunal shall also find that the merger or proposed merger prevents or lessens, or is likely to prevent or lessen, competition substantially, unless the contrary is proved on a balance of probabilities by the parties to the merger or proposed merger.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Significant increase in concentration or market share
Subsection.Text: A merger or proposed merger results or is likely to result in a significant increase in concentration or market share if, in any relevant market, as a result of the merger or proposed merger,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the concentration index increases or is likely to increase by more than 100; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the concentration index is or is likely to be more than 1,800, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the market share of the parties to the merger or proposed merger is or is likely to be more than 30%.

Subsection.Text: In subsection (3), concentration index means, in any relevant market, the sum of the squares of the market shares of the suppliers or customers.
The Governor in Council may by regulation prescribe different values than those provided in subsection (3).
Paragraph 93(g.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: network effects within a market;

Subsection.Text: Section 93 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (g.3) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (g.4)
Paragraph.Text: the change in concentration or market share that the merger or proposed merger has brought about or is likely to bring about;

Paragraph.Label: (g.5)
Paragraph.Text: any likelihood that the merger or proposed merger will or would result in express or tacit coordination between competitors in a market; and

Section.Text: Section 97 of the Act is replaced by the following:
No application may be made under section 92,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a merger that was the subject of a request for a certificate under section 102 or a notification under section 114, more than one year after the merger has been substantially completed; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of any other merger, more than three years after the merger has been substantially completed.

Section.Text: Paragraph 98(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order against that person has been made under section 79 or 90.1.

Section.Text: Section 100 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Effect of application for interim order
Subsection.Text: If an application for an interim order is made under subsection (1) in respect of a proposed merger, the merger shall not be completed until the application has been disposed of by the Tribunal.
Subsections 103.1(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Leave to make application under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1

Subsection.Text: Any person may apply to the Tribunal for leave to make an application under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1. The application for leave must be accompanied by an affidavit setting out the facts in support of the person’s application under that section.
The applicant must serve a copy of the application for leave on the Commissioner and any person against whom the order is sought under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1, as the case may be.
Paragraph 103.1(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: was the subject of an inquiry that has been discontinued because of a settlement between the Commissioner and the person against whom the order is sought under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 103.1(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Tribunal is not to consider an application for leave respecting a matter described in paragraph (3)(a) or (b) or a matter that is the subject of an application already submitted to the Tribunal by the Commissioner under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1.
Subsection 103.1(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Tribunal may grant leave to make an application under section 74.1 if it is satisfied that it is in the public interest to do so.

The Tribunal may grant leave to make an application under section 75, 77, 79 or 90.1 if it has reason to believe that the applicant is directly and substantially affected in the whole or part of the applicant’s business by any conduct referred to in one of those sections that could be subject to an order under that section or if it is satisfied that it is in the public interest to do so.
Section 103.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7.1):

The Tribunal is not to consider an application for leave in respect of an application under section 90.1 that relates to an agreement or arrangement for which a certificate issued under subsection 124.3(1) is valid and registered.
Subsection 103.1(8) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Time and conditions for making application
Subsection.Text: The Tribunal may set the time within which and the conditions subject to which an application under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1 must be made. The application must be made no more than one year after the practice or conduct that is the subject of the application has ceased.
Subsection 103.1(10) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commissioner may not make an application for an order under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1 on the basis of the same or substantially the same facts as are alleged in a matter for which the Tribunal has granted leave under subsection (6.1), (7) or (7.1), if the person granted leave has already applied to the Tribunal under one of those sections.

Section.Text: Section 103.2 of the Act is replaced by the following
If a person granted leave under subsection 103.1(6.1), (7) or (7.1) makes an application under section 74.1, 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1, the Commissioner may intervene in the proceedings.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 104(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an application has been made for an order under this Part, other than an interim order under section 100 or 103.3, the Tribunal, on application by the Commissioner or a person who has made an application under section 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1, may issue any interim order that it considers appropriate, having regard to the principles ordinarily considered by superior courts when granting interlocutory or injunctive relief.
Section 104 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Effect of application for interim order
Subsection.Text: If an application for an interim order is made under subsection (1) in respect of a proposed merger, the merger shall not be completed until the application has been disposed of by the Tribunal.

Section.Text: Subsection 106.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Consent agreement — parties to a private action

Subsection.Text: If a person granted leave under section 103.1 makes an application to the Tribunal for an order under section 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1 and the terms of the order are agreed to by the person in respect of whom the order is sought and consistent with the provisions of this Act, a consent agreement may be filed with the Tribunal for registration.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 106.1:

Section.MarginalNote: Failure to comply with consent agreement

Subsection.Text: If, on application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal determines that a person, without good and sufficient cause, the proof of which lies on the person, has failed to comply or is likely to fail to comply with a consent agreement registered under subsection 105(3) or 106.1(4), the Tribunal may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prohibit the person from doing anything that, in the Tribunal’s opinion, may constitute a failure to comply with the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to take any action that is necessary to comply with the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to pay, in any manner that the Tribunal specifies, an administrative monetary penalty in an amount not exceeding $10,000 for each day on which they fail to comply with the agreement, determined by the Tribunal after taking into account any evidence of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s financial position,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s history of compliance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the duration of the period of non-compliance, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any other relevant factor; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: grant any other relief that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: The terms of an order under paragraph (1)(c) are to be determined with a view to promoting conduct by the person that is in conformity with the purposes of this Act and not with a view to punishment.
The administrative monetary penalty imposed under paragraph (1)(c) is a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such from the person in a court of competent jurisdiction.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 106.2:

Subsection.Text: If a person granted leave under section 103.1 makes an application to the Tribunal for an order under section 75, 76, 77, 79 or 90.1 and the person discontinues the application by reason of having entered into an agreement with any other person, the parties to the agreement must serve a copy of it on the Commissioner within 10 days after the day on which it was entered into.
On application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal may vary or rescind the agreement if it finds that the agreement has or is likely to have anti-competitive effects.
The Commissioner must give notice of an application under subsection (2) to the parties to the agreement.

If, on application by the Commissioner, the Tribunal determines that a person, without good and sufficient cause, the proof of which lies on the person, has failed to serve a copy of an agreement on the Commissioner in accordance with subsection 106.3(1), the Tribunal may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to serve the Commissioner with a copy of the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: issue an interim order prohibiting any person from doing anything that, in the Tribunal’s opinion, may constitute or be directed toward the implementation of the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: order the person to pay, in any manner that the Tribunal specifies, an administrative monetary penalty in an amount not exceeding $10,000 for each day on which they fail to serve a copy of the agreement on the Commissioner, determined by the Tribunal after taking into account any evidence of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s financial position,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person’s history of compliance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the duration of the period of non-compliance, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any other relevant factor; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: grant any other relief that the Tribunal considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: The terms of an order under paragraph (1)(c) are to be determined with a view to promoting conduct by the person that is in conformity with the purposes of this Act and not with a view to punishment.
The administrative monetary penalty imposed under paragraph (1)(c) is a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered as such from the person in a court of competent jurisdiction.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 107:
The following definitions apply in this Part:

Definition.Text: court means the Federal Court or the superior court of a province. (tribunal)

reprisal action means an action taken by a person to penalize, punish, discipline, harass or disadvantage another person because of that person’s communications with the Commissioner or because that person has cooperated, testified or assisted, or has expressed an intention to cooperate, testify or assist in an investigation or proceeding under this Act. (représailles)

Section.Text: If, following an application by the Commissioner or a person directly and substantially affected by an alleged reprisal action, a court concludes that a person is engaging, has engaged or is likely to engage in a reprisal action, it may make an order prohibiting the person from engaging in that action.
