Paragraph.Label: (b.3)
Paragraph.Text: if the Minister agrees to be responsible for its custody and management, forfeited under any Act of Parliament, other than under subsection 14(5) or 39.03(5) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act or subparagraph 715.34(1)(e)(i) of the Criminal Code or forfeited under any Act of the legislature of a province; or

Heading.TitleText: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) Suspicious Transaction Reporting Regulations

Section.Text: Section 9 of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) Suspicious Transaction Reporting Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 11, a report made under section 7 of the Act concerning a financial transaction or attempted financial transaction in respect of which there are reasonable grounds to suspect that the transaction or attempted transaction is related to the commission of a money laundering offence, a terrorist activity financing offence or a sanctions evasion offence shall contain the information set out in Schedule 1.

The person or entity shall send the report to the Centre as soon as practicable after they have taken measures that enable them to establish that there are reasonable grounds to suspect that the transaction or attempted transaction is related to the commission of a money laundering offence, a terrorist activity financing offence or a sanctions evasion offence.

Section.Text: Item 1 of Part G of Schedule 1 to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Item.Text: Detailed description of grounds to suspect that transaction or attempted transaction is related to the commission or attempted commission of a money laundering offence, a terrorist activity financing offence or a sanctions evasion offence

Subsection.Text: In subsections (2) and (3), other Act means the Budget Implementation Act, 2023, No. 1.
If section 181 of the other Act comes into force before section 299 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 299 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 7.1(1)(b) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order or regulation made under the United Nations Act;

Subsection.Text: If section 181 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 299 of this Act, then that section 299 is deemed to have come into force before that section 181.
Subsection 278(1) and sections 285, 296, 297, 301 and 302 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 278(3) and section 279 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 280, 303 and 304 come into force on the 60th day after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Section.Text: Subsection 83.13(11) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 462.32(4), sections 462.34 to 462.35 and 462.4, subsection 487(3) and section 488 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to a warrant issued under paragraph (1)(a). Any peace officer who executes the warrant must have authority to act as a peace officer in the place where it is executed.

Section.Text: Section 462.31 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.3), in a prosecution for an offence under subsection (1), it is not necessary for the prosecutor to prove that the accused knew, believed they knew or was reckless as to the specific nature of the designated offence.

Subject to subsection (1.3), the court may infer that an accused had the knowledge or belief or demonstrated the recklessness referred to in subsection (1) if it is satisfied, given the circumstances of the offence, that the manner in which the accused dealt with the property or its proceeds is markedly unusual or the accused’s dealings are inconsistent with lawful activities typical of the sector in which they take place, including business activities.
Subsections (1.1) and (1.2) do not apply in cases where the accused is also charged with the designated offence.
Subsection 462.32(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (3), if a judge, on application of the Attorney General, is satisfied by information on oath in Form 1 that there are reasonable grounds to believe that there is in any building, receptacle or place any property that is proceeds of crime, the judge may issue a warrant authorizing a person named in the warrant or a peace officer to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: search the building, receptacle or place for that property; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: seize that property and any other property that that person or peace officer believes, on reasonable grounds, is proceeds of crime.

Subsection.Text: Section 462.32 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
A warrant issued under subsection (1) may be subject to any reasonable conditions that the judge thinks fit.
Subsection 462.32(6) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 462.321(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, on an application of the Attorney General, a judge is satisfied by information on oath in Form 1, varied to suit the case, that there are reasonable grounds to believe that any digital assets, including virtual currency, are proceeds of crime, the judge may issue a warrant authorizing a person named in the warrant or a peace officer to
Paragraph 462.321(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: seize — including by taking control of the right to access — the digital assets, as well as any other digital assets found during that search that the person or peace officer believes, on reasonable grounds, are proceeds of crime.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 462.321(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
A warrant issued under subsection (1) may be subject to any reasonable conditions that the judge thinks fit.
Section 462.321 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

A warrant issued under subsection (1) may be executed at any place in Canada. Any peace officer who executes the warrant must have authority to act as a peace officer in the place where it is executed.
Subsection 462.321(7) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 462.33(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the grounds for the belief that the property is proceeds of crime;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 462.33(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A judge who hears an application for a restraint order made under subsection (1) may, if the judge is satisfied that there are reasonable grounds to believe that there exists any property that is proceeds of crime, make an order prohibiting any person from disposing of, or otherwise dealing with any interest in, the property specified in the order otherwise than in the manner that may be specified in the order.
Subsection 462.33(7) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 487.018(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the account number of a person named in the order or the name of a person whose account number is specified in the order, as well as, in the case of digital assets, including virtual currency, the name and account number of a person whose identifier associated with digital assets is specified in the order;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 487.018(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of confirming the identity of a person who is named or whose account number or identifier associated with digital assets is specified in the order, the order may also require the institution, person or entity to prepare and produce a document setting out the following data that is in their possession or control:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the date of birth of a person who is named or whose account number or identifier associated with digital assets is specified in the order;

Section.Text: Form 1 of Part XXVIII of the Act is amended by replacing the references after the heading “FORM 1” with the following:
Paragraph 13(3)(b) of the Seized Property Management Act is repealed.
Section 16 of the Act is replaced by the following:

At the prescribed times, all amounts credited to the Proceeds Account that are not shared under sections 10 and 11, less the amounts that are reserved for future losses and for ongoing expenses, shall be credited to the prescribed account in the accounts of Canada.
Subparagraph 5(b)(ii) of the Forfeited Property Sharing Regulations is repealed.
This Subdivision comes into force on the 90th day after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Section 42 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Payments under Parts I, I.1, II and V.1
Section.Text: The Minister shall publish the following information on a Government of Canada website as soon as feasible after the payment of an amount under Part I, I.1, II or V.1:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the province to which the payment was made; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the date of the payment.

Section.Text: Section 318 is deemed to have come into force on June 22, 2023.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 10
Heading.TitleText: Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(1) of the Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act is replaced by the following:
The Board shall be managed by a board of 13 directors, including the Chairperson.
Subsection 6(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is a member of an advisory committee established under section 41 of the Public Service Superannuation Act, section 49.1 of the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act or section 25.1 of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act;

Section.Text: Section 9 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: For two of the directors, the Minister’s recommendation under subsection (1) shall be made from among the candidates who are included on the list in accordance with subsection 10(6).

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.Text: When including a candidate who the Minister may recommend under subsection 9(3) on a list of qualified candidates for proposed appointment as directors, the nominating committee shall consult the portion of the National Joint Council of the Public Service that represents employees and shall have regard to any factors for consideration provided by that portion of the National Joint Council.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 11
Heading.TitleText: Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Act

Section.Text: The Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities

Provision.Text: Whereas public infrastructure and housing are essential for communities to be complete, inclusive and environmentally sustainable;
Whereas these types of communities foster a stronger national economy in which the people of Canada can prosper and thrive;
Whereas advancing public infrastructure and housing outcomes is best achieved through cooperation between governments as well as the meaningful involvement of local communities;
Whereas effective support for infrastructure plays a key role in improving housing outcomes;
And whereas promoting the use of innovative financial tools helps attract investment from the private sector and institutional investors in public infrastructure projects;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Act.
In this Act, Department means the department established under section 3.

Heading.TitleText: Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities

Section.Text: A department of the Government of Canada, called the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities, is established and is to be presided over by the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities.
The Governor in Council may appoint a Deputy Minister of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities to hold office during pleasure and to be the deputy head of the Department.
The Minister of Infrastructure and Communities, appointed by commission under the Great Seal, holds office during pleasure and has the management and direction of the Department.

Subsection.Text: The powers, duties and functions of the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities extend to and include all matters relating to public infrastructure over which Parliament has jurisdiction and that are not by law assigned to any other department, board or agency of the Government of Canada.

Without restricting the generality of subsection (1), the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities’ duties and functions include supporting and promoting infrastructure projects and initiatives that are in the public interest in order to foster the prosperity, inclusivity and environmental sustainability of communities.

Section.Text: A Minister of Housing may be appointed by commission under the Great Seal to hold office during pleasure.

Subsection.Text: The powers, duties and functions of the Minister of Housing extend to and include all matters relating to housing and the reduction and prevention of homelessness over which Parliament has jurisdiction and that are not by law assigned to any other Minister, department, board or agency of the Government of Canada.

Without restricting the generality of subsection (1), the Minister of Housing’s duties and functions include advancing national housing priorities and reducing and preventing homelessness to foster the prosperity, inclusivity and environmental sustainability of communities.

Section.MarginalNote: Use of departmental services and facilities
Section.Text: The Minister of Housing is to make use of the Department services and facilities and may authorize employees of the Department to exercise any power or perform any duty or function of the Minister of Housing.
If no Minister is appointed under section 7,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities is to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the Minister of Housing; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every reference to the Minister of Housing in any Act of Parliament or in any order, regulation or other instrument made under an Act of Parliament is to be read as a reference to the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities, unless the context otherwise requires.

Section.Text: The Minister of Infrastructure and Communities or the Minister of Housing, as the case may be, may, in exercising their powers and performing their duties and functions,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish, recommend, coordinate and implement initiatives, programs and projects;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make grants and contributions;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: collaborate or enter into agreements or other arrangements with other federal, provincial or territorial departments, boards and agencies, local governments, Indigenous bodies or any institution or person;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: undertake, coordinate and promote research activities; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subject to the Statistics Act, collect, analyze, interpret, publish or distribute information.

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Infrastructure and Communities or the Minister of Housing, as the case may be, may establish advisory and other committees and provide for their membership, duties, functions and operation.

The Minister of Infrastructure and Communities or the Minister of Housing, as the case may be, may fix the remuneration that members of a committee are to be paid for the performance of their duties and functions.

Members of a committee are entitled to be reimbursed, in accordance with Treasury Board directives, for the travel, living and other expenses incurred for the performance of their duties and functions while absent from their ordinary place of residence.

Any person who, immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, holds the office of Deputy Head of Infrastructure and Communities, styled as Deputy Minister of Infrastructure and Communities, is deemed, as of that day, to have been appointed as the Deputy Minister referred to in section 4 of the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Act, as enacted by section 323.

Nothing in the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Act is to be construed as affecting the status of any person who, immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, occupies or is assigned to a position in the Office of Infrastructure of Canada, except that, as of that day, the person occupies or is assigned to their position in the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities.

Section.Text: Any amount that is appropriated by an Act of Parliament for the fiscal year in which this section comes into force to defray the expenditures of the public service of Canada within the Office of Infrastructure of Canada and that is unexpended on the day on which this section comes into force is deemed to be an amount appropriated to defray the expenditures of the public service of Canada within the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities.

On the day on which this section comes into force, every reference to the Office of Infrastructure of Canada in any agreement, contract, instrument or act or other document is to be read as a reference to the Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities, unless the context requires otherwise.
Schedule I to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order under the heading “Departments and Ministries of State”:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère du Logement, de l’Infrastructure et des Collectivités

Section.Text: Schedule I to the Act is amended by striking out the following under the heading “Other Government Institutions”:
Schedule I to the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère du Logement, de l’Infrastructure et des Collectivités

Section.Text: Schedule I.1 to the Act is amended by striking out, in column I, the reference to

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and the corresponding reference in column II to “Minister of Infrastructure and Communities”.

Section.Text: Schedule IV to the Act is amended by striking out the following:
Part I of Schedule VI to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère du Logement, de l’Infrastructure et des Collectivités

Section.Text: Part II of Schedule VI to the Act is amended by striking out, in column I, the reference to

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and the corresponding reference in column II to “Deputy Head”.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Privacy Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order under the heading “Departments and Ministries of State”:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère du Logement, de l’Infrastructure et des Collectivités

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is amended by striking out the following under the heading “Other Government Institutions”:
Subsection 4.1(3) of the Salaries Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (z.25):

Paragraph.Label: (z.26)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Housing;

Section.Text: Schedule I to the Public Sector Compensation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order under the heading “Departments”:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Department of Housing, Infrastructure and Communities
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Ministère du Logement, de l’Infrastructure et des Collectivités

Heading.TitleText: Keeping Canada’s Economy and Jobs Growing Act

Section.Text: Subsection 161(1) of the Keeping Canada’s Economy and Jobs Growing Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund for each fiscal year beginning on or after April 1, 2014, on the requisition of the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities or of the Minister of Indigenous Services, in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Treasury Board, a sum of not more than the amount determined in accordance with subsection (2) to provinces, territories, municipalities, municipal associations, provincial, territorial and municipal entities and First Nations for the purpose of municipal, regional and First Nations infrastructure.

Section.Text: Section 12 of the National Housing Strategy Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister is to provide the National Housing Council with any administrative services and facilities that are necessary to assist the Council in performing its duties and functions.
The Canada Strategic Infrastructure Fund Act, section 47 of chapter 9 of the Statutes of Canada, 2002, is repealed.

Section.MarginalNote: Second anniversary or order in council

Subsection.Text: Section 339 comes into force on the second anniversary of the day on which this Act receives royal assent or on an earlier day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 340 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 12
Heading.TitleText: Measures Related to Placement or Arrival of Children

Subsection.Text: Section 10 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (11):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extension of benefit period — placement or arrival delayed
Subsection.Text: If the placement or arrival of the child or children referred to in subsection 22.1(1) is delayed, the benefit period is extended by the number of weeks during which the placement or arrival is delayed.
Paragraph 10(13.01)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: benefits were not paid for any reason mentioned in paragraph 12(3)(a), (a.1), (c), (d), (e) or (f).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 12(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: because the claimant is carrying out the responsibilities described in subsection 22.1(1) is 15;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 12(4) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: for carrying out the responsibilities described in subsection 22.1(1) in relation to the placement of one or more children for the purpose of adoption as a result of a single placement or the arrival of one or more new-born children as a result of a single pregnancy is 15; and

Section.Text: Subsection 18(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A claimant to whom benefits are payable under any of sections 22.1 to 23.3 is not disentitled under paragraph (1)(b) for failing to prove that they would have been available for work were it not for the illness, injury or quarantine.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 22:

Section.MarginalNote: Benefit for responsibilities related to child’s placement or arrival

Subsection.Text: Despite section 18, but subject to this section, benefits are payable to a major attachment claimant for carrying out responsibilities related to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the placement with the claimant of one or more children for the purpose of adoption under the laws governing adoption in the province in which the claimant resides; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the arrival of one or more new-born children of the claimant into the claimant’s care, in the case where the person who will be giving or gave birth to the child or children is not, or is not intended to be, a parent of the child or children.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Weeks for which benefits may be paid
Subsection.Text: Subject to section 12, benefits under this section are payable for each week of unemployment in the period

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that begins the earlier of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: five weeks before the week in which the placement of the child or children with the claimant for the purpose of adoption is expected or the new-born child or children of the claimant are expected to arrive into the claimant’s care, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the week in which the child or children are actually placed with the claimant for the purpose of adoption or the new-born child or children of the claimant actually arrive into the claimant’s care; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that ends 17 weeks after the week in which the child or children are actually placed with the claimant for the purpose of adoption or the new-born child or children of the claimant actually arrive into the claimant’s care.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Limitation — delay of placement or arrival
Subsection.Text: If the placement or arrival of the child or children referred to in subsection (1) is delayed, the period referred to in subsection (2) must not, subject to any extension under subsection (4), exceed 52 weeks after the week in which the placement or arrival was expected.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extension of period — children in hospital
Subsection.Text: If the child or children referred to in subsection (1) are hospitalized during the period that begins the week referred to in subparagraph (2)(a)(ii) and that ends 17 weeks after that week, the period referred to in subsection (2) is extended by the number of weeks during which the child or children are hospitalized.
The extended period shall end no later than 52 weeks after the week referred to in subparagraph (2)(a)(ii).

If benefits are payable to a claimant for the reasons set out in this section and any allowances, money or other benefits are payable to the claimant for the same reasons under a provincial law, the benefits payable to the claimant under this Act are to be reduced or eliminated as prescribed.
For the purposes of section 13, the provisions of section 18 do not apply to the week that immediately precedes the period described in subsection (2).

If two major attachment claimants each make a claim for benefits under this section — or if one major attachment claimant makes a claim for benefits under this section and an individual makes a claim for benefits under section 152.041 — in respect of the same child or children, the weeks of benefits payable under this section, under section 152.041 or under both those sections may be divided between them up to a maximum of 15. If they cannot agree, the weeks of benefits are to be divided in accordance with the prescribed rules.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum number of weeks that can be divided
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, if, in respect of the same child or children, a major attachment claimant makes a claim for benefits under this section and an individual makes a claim for benefits under section 152.041, the total number of weeks of benefits payable under this section and section 152.041 that may be divided between them may not exceed 15.

A major attachment claimant who makes a claim for benefits under this section may have their waiting period deferred until they make another claim for benefits in the same benefit period, otherwise than under this section, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant has already made a claim for benefits under this section in respect of the same child or children and has served the waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: another major attachment claimant has made a claim for benefits under this section in respect of the same child or children and that other claimant has served or is serving their waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: another major attachment claimant is making a claim for benefits under this section in respect of the same child or children at the same time as the claimant and that other claimant elects to serve the waiting period; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant or another major attachment claimant meets the prescribed requirements.

Subsection.Text: If a major attachment claimant makes a claim under this section and an individual makes a claim under section 152.041 in respect of the same child or children and one of them has served or elected to serve their waiting period, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the major attachment claimant is not the one who served or elected to serve the waiting period, that claimant is not required to serve a waiting period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the individual is not the one who served or elected to serve the waiting period, that claimant may have their waiting period deferred in accordance with section 152.041.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23(3.21)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: benefits were not paid for any reason mentioned in paragraph 12(3)(a), (a.1), (c), (d), (e) or (f).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 23(5) of the Act before paragraph (d) is replaced by the following:

A major attachment claimant who makes a claim for benefits under this section may have their waiting period deferred until they make another claim for benefits in the same benefit period, otherwise than under this section or section 22 or 22.1, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant has already made a claim for benefits under this section or section 22 or 22.1 in respect of the same child or children and has served the waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: another major attachment claimant has made a claim for benefits under this section or section 22 or 22.1 in respect of the same child or children and that other claimant has served or is serving their waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: another major attachment claimant is making a claim for benefits under this section or section 22 or 22.1 in respect of the same child or children at the same time as the claimant and that other claimant elects to serve the waiting period; or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 23(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If a major attachment claimant makes a claim under this section or section 22 or 22.1 and an individual makes a claim under section 152.04, 152.041 or 152.05 in respect of the same child or children and one of them has served or elected to serve their waiting period, then
Paragraph 23(6)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the individual is not the one who served or elected to serve the waiting period, that claimant may have their waiting period deferred in accordance with section 152.041 or 152.05, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Paragraph 54(f.7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 69(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the payment of any allowances, money or other benefits because of illness, injury, quarantine, pregnancy, responsibilities related to a child’s placement or arrival, child care, compassionate care, a child’s critical illness or an adult’s critical illness under a plan that covers insured persons employed by the employer, other than one established under a provincial law, would have the effect of reducing the special benefits payable to the insured persons; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 69(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commission shall, with the approval of the Governor in Council, make regulations to provide a system for reducing the employer’s and employee’s premiums, the premiums under Part VII.1 or all those premiums, when the payment of any allowances, money or other benefits because of illness, injury, quarantine, pregnancy, responsibilities related to a child’s placement or arrival, child care, compassionate care, a child’s critical illness or an adult’s critical illness under a provincial law to insured persons or to self-employed persons, as the case may be, would have the effect of reducing or eliminating the special benefits payable to those insured persons or the benefits payable to those self-employed persons.

Section.Text: Subsection 152.03(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A self-employed person to whom benefits are payable under any of sections 152.041 to 152.062 is entitled to benefits under subsection (1) even though the person did not cease to work as a self-employed person because of a prescribed illness, injury or quarantine and would not be working even without the illness, injury or quarantine.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 152.04:

Section.MarginalNote: Benefit for responsibilities related to child’s placement or arrival

Subsection.Text: Subject to this Part, benefits are payable to a self-employed person for carrying out responsibilities related to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the placement with the self-employed person of one or more children for the purpose of adoption under the laws governing adoption in the province in which the person resides; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the arrival of one or more new-born children of the self-employed person into the self-employed person’s care, in the case where the person who will be giving or gave birth to the child or children is not, or is not intended to be, a parent of the child or children.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Weeks for which benefits may be paid
Subsection.Text: Subject to section 152.14, benefits under this section are payable for each week of unemployment in the period

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that begins the earlier of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: five weeks before the week in which the placement of the child or children with the self-employed person for the purpose of adoption is expected or the new-born child or children of the self-employed person are expected to arrive into the self-employed person’s care, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the week in which the child or children are actually placed with the self-employed person for the purpose of adoption or the new-born child or children of the self-employed person actually arrive into the self-employed person’s care; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that ends 17 weeks after the week in which the child or children are actually placed with the self-employed person for the purpose of adoption or the new-born child or children of the self-employed person actually arrive into the self-employed person’s care.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Limitation — delay of placement or arrival
Subsection.Text: If the placement or arrival of the child or children referred to in subsection (1) is delayed, the period referred to in subsection (2) must not, subject to any extension under subsection (4), exceed 52 weeks after the week in which the placement or arrival was expected.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extension of period — children in hospital
Subsection.Text: If the child or children referred to in subsection (1) are hospitalized during the period that begins the week referred to in subparagraph (2)(a)(ii) and that ends 17 weeks after that week, the period referred to in subsection (2) is extended by the number of weeks during which the child or children are hospitalized.
The extended period shall end no later than 52 weeks after the week referred to in subparagraph (2)(a)(ii).

If benefits are payable to a self-employed person for the reasons set out in this section and any allowances, money or other benefits are payable to the self-employed person for the same reasons under a provincial law, the benefits payable to the self-employed person under this Part are to be reduced or eliminated as prescribed.
With regard to serving the waiting period under section 152.15, the week that immediately precedes the period described in subsection (2) is deemed to be a week that is included in that period.

If two self-employed persons each make a claim for benefits under this section — or if one self-employed person makes a claim for benefits under this section and another person makes a claim for benefits under section 22.1 — in respect of the same child or children, the weeks of benefits payable under this section, under section 22.1 or under both those sections may be divided between them up to a maximum of 15. If they cannot agree, the weeks of benefits are to be divided in accordance with the prescribed rules.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum number of weeks that can be divided
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, if, in respect of the same child or children, a self-employed person makes a claim for benefits under this section and another person makes a claim for benefits under section 22.1, the total number of weeks of benefits payable under this section and section 22.1 that may be divided between them may not exceed 15.

A self-employed person who makes a claim for benefits under this section may have their waiting period deferred until they make another claim for benefits in the same benefit period, otherwise than under this section, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the self-employed person has already made a claim for benefits under this section in respect of the same child or children and has served the waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: another self-employed person has made a claim for benefits under this section in respect of the same child or children and that other self-employed person has served or is serving their waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: another self-employed person is making a claim for benefits under this section in respect of the same child or children at the same time as the self-employed person and that other self-employed person elects to serve the waiting period; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the self-employed person or another self-employed person meets the prescribed requirements.

Subsection.Text: If a self-employed person makes a claim under this section and an another person makes a claim under section 22.1 in respect of the same child or children and one of them has served or elected to serve their waiting period, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the self-employed person is not the one who served or elected to serve the waiting period, the self-employed person is not required to serve a waiting period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the person making the claim under section 22.1 is not the one who served or elected to serve the waiting period, the person may have their waiting period deferred in accordance with section 22.1.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 152.05(5.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extension of period — reason mentioned in paragraph 152.14(1)(b)
Subsection.Text: If, during a self-employed person’s benefit period, benefits were not paid for any reason mentioned in paragraph 152.14(1)(a), (a.1), (c), (d), (e) or (f) and benefits were paid to the person for the reason mentioned in paragraph 152.14(1)(b) in the case where the applicable maximum number of weeks is established under subparagraph 152.14(1)(b)(ii), the period referred to in subsection (2) is extended by 26 weeks so that benefits may be paid up to that maximum number of weeks.
The portion of subsection 152.05(14) of the Act before paragraph (d) is replaced by the following:

A self-employed person who makes a claim for benefits under this section may have their waiting period deferred until they make another claim for benefits in the same benefit period, otherwise than under this section or section 152.04 or 152.041, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the self-employed person has already made a claim for benefits under this section or section 152.04 or 152.041 in respect of the same child or children and has served the waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: another self-employed person has made a claim for benefits under this section or section 152.04 or 152.041 in respect of the same child or children and that other self-employed person has served or is serving their waiting period;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: another self-employed person is making a claim for benefits under this section or section 152.04 or 152.041 in respect of the same child or children at the same time as the self-employed person and that other self-employed person elects to serve the waiting period; or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 152.05(15) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If a self-employed person makes a claim under this section or section 152.04 or 152.041 and another person makes a claim under section 22, 22.1 or 23 in respect of the same child or children and one of them has served or elected to serve their waiting period, then
Paragraph 152.05(15)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the person making the claim under section 22, 22.1 or 23 is not the one who served or elected to serve the waiting period, the person may have their waiting period deferred in accordance with section 22.1 or 23, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Subsection 152.09(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: carrying out the responsibilities described in subsection 152.041(1);

Subsection.Text: Section 152.11 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (12):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extension of benefit period — placement or arrival delayed
Subsection.Text: If the placement or arrival of the child or children referred to in subsection 152.041(1) is delayed, the benefit period is extended by the number of weeks during which the placement or arrival is delayed.
Subsection 152.11(14.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extension of benefit period — reason mentioned in paragraph 152.14(1)(b)
Subsection.Text: If, during a self-employed person’s benefit period, benefits were not paid for any reason mentioned in paragraph 152.14(1)(a), (a.1), (c), (d), (e) or (f), and benefits were paid to the person for the reason mentioned in paragraph 152.14(1)(b) in the case where the applicable maximum number of weeks is established under subparagraph 152.14(1)(b)(ii), the benefit period is extended by 26 weeks so that benefits may be paid up to that maximum number of weeks.
Subsection 152.14(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: because the self-employed person is carrying out the responsibilities described in subsection 152.041(1) is 15;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 152.14(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by replacing paragraph (a) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: for carrying out the responsibilities described in subsection 152.041(1) in relation to the placement of one or more children for the purpose of adoption as a result of a single placement or the arrival of one or more new-born children as a result of a single pregnancy is 15; and

Section.MarginalNote: Benefit for responsibilities related to child’s placement or arrival
Section.Text: The Employment Insurance Act, as it read immediately before the day on which sections 345 and 350 come into force, continues to apply to a claimant for the purpose of paying benefits under that Act in respect of a child or children who have, before that day,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: been placed with the claimant for the purpose of adoption under the laws governing adoption in the province in which the claimant resides; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: arrived into the care of the claimant.

Section.Text: Subsection 187.1(2) of the Canada Labour Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an employee interrupts a vacation to take leave under any of sections 205.1, 206 to 206.1 and 206.3 to 206.9 and resumes the vacation immediately at the end of that leave, section 209.1 applies to them as if they did not resume the vacation before returning to work.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 206:

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: placement means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the placement of a child into the actual care of an employee for the purposes of adoption under the laws governing adoption in the province in which the employee resides;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the arrival of a new-born child of an employee into the employee’s actual care, in the case where the person who gave birth to the child is not, or is not intended to be, a parent of the child; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other case prescribed by regulation. (placement)

Definition.Text: week means the period between midnight on Saturday and midnight on the immediately following Saturday. (semaine)

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (7) and (8), every employee is entitled to and shall be granted a leave of absence from employment of up to 16 weeks for carrying out responsibilities related to a placement.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Period when leave may be taken
Subsection.Text: The leave of absence may only be taken during the period

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: beginning no earlier than six weeks before the week of the estimated date of the placement or, if the actual date of the placement is earlier than the estimated date, no earlier than the week of that actual date; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ending no later than 17 weeks following the week of the actual date of that placement.

Subsection.Text: If the placement is delayed, the period referred to in subsection (3) must not, subject to any extension under subsection (5), end later than 52 weeks following the week of the estimated date referred to in paragraph (3)(a).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extension of period — child in hospital
Subsection.Text: If, after placement, the child is hospitalized during the period referred to in subsection (3), the period is extended by the number of weeks during which the child is hospitalized.
An extension under subsection (5) must not result in the period referred to in subsection (3) ending later than 52 weeks following the week of the actual date of the placement.
The aggregate amount of leave that may be taken by more than one employee under this section in respect of the same placement must not exceed 16 weeks.

If, during a leave of absence under this section, the employee is informed that the placement will not occur, the leave may continue until the end of the week after the week in which the employee is so informed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 206.2:

Section.MarginalNote: Aggregate leave — leave for placement of child and parental leave
Section.Text: The aggregate amount of leave that may be taken by more than one employee under sections 206.01 and 206.1 in respect of the same child shall not exceed 85 weeks, but the aggregate amount of leave that may be taken by one employee under those sections in respect of the same child shall not exceed 77 weeks.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 207(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every employee who intends to take a leave of absence from employment under any of sections 206 to 206.1 shall
Subsection 207(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every employee who intends to take or who is on a leave of absence from employment under any of sections 206 to 206.1 shall provide the employer with notice in writing of at least four weeks of any change in the length of leave intended to be taken, unless there is a valid reason why that notice cannot be given, in which case the employee shall provide the employer with notice in writing as soon as possible.

Section.Text: Section 207.01 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to the regulations, a leave of absence under any of sections 206.01 and 206.3 to 206.5 may only be taken in one or more periods of not less than one week’s duration.
Subsection 207.02(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An employee may interrupt a leave of absence referred to in any of sections 206.01 and 206.3 to 206.5 in order to be absent due to a reason referred to in subsection 239(1) or 239.1(1).
Subsection 207.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Notification to employer — interruption for child’s hospitalization

Subsection.Text: An employee who intends to interrupt their maternity or parental leave or their leave for the placement of a child in order to return to work as a result of the hospitalization of their child shall provide the employer with a notice in writing of the interruption as soon as possible.
Subsection 207.2(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the employer refuses the interruption or does not advise the employee within the week referred to in subsection (2), the leave under any of sections 206 to 206.1 is extended by the number of weeks during which the child is hospitalized. The aggregate amounts of leave referred to in subsections 206.01(7) and 206.1(3) and sections 206.2 and 206.21 are extended by the same number of weeks.
Subsection 207.2(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An employee who intends to return to their leave after the interruption shall, as soon as possible, advise the employer in writing of the date on which the leave is to resume.
Paragraph 209.4(a.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the maximum number of periods of leave of absence that an employee may take under any of sections 206.01 and 206.3 to 206.5;

Subsection.Text: Section 209.4 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing cases for the purposes of paragraph (c) of the definition placement in subsection 206.01(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: defining, for the purposes of section 206.01, any word or expression that is used but not defined in that section;

Subsection.Text: In this section, Act means the Canada Labour Code.

An employee who, on the day on which section 357 comes into force, is on parental leave under section 206.1 of the Act and is eligible for leave for the placement of a child under section 206.01 of the Act may interrupt their parental leave to take leave for the placement of a child. Their parental leave resumes immediately after the interruption ends.
Section 207.1 of the Act applies, with any necessary modifications, with respect to an interruption under subsection (2).
Words and expressions used in this section have the same meaning as in the Act.

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
